text:
"#FreeAafiaNow #BringAafiaHome
The US war on Muslim women.
The Story of Aafia Siddiqui . A Mother, A Sister, A Prisoner. The most oppressed woman in the world.

She was kidnapped. Sold. Raped. Her children were torn from her arms and sold alongside her, and what followed is one of the most harrowing stories of injustice in modern history.
Aafia Siddiqui, a Pakistani neuroscientist and mother of three, was abducted along with her young children. The Pakistani government sold her and her babies to the CIA for $55,000, a transaction that treated a mother and her children as commodities.

What was done to those children defies imagination.

Her infant son, Sulaiman, was just six months old when a CIA agent killed him, slamming his head to the ground in front of his six-year-old brother, Ahmed. Ahmed was then taken to Afghanistan, tortured, and imprisoned for five years.

Her daughter, Maryam, was only three years old. She was forcibly placed with a Christian family who changed her name and kept her for seven years.

As for Aafia herself, she was tortured, raped, and is currently serving an 86-year sentence in a US federal prison. Never once charged with terrorism. Never convicted of a violent crime.

This is United States terrorism on Muslim women. A mother, a daughter, a sister, targeted, brutalised, and discarded. Her only crime was her faith and her identity.

And what is perhaps most shocking is the silence.

The silence of human rights organizations. The silence of NGOs. The silence of the international community, the very institutions that exist to speak for the voiceless, that march and campaign and raise millions in the name of justice. Where are they for Aafia? Where are they for her children?

Their silence is not neutrality. Their silence is complicity.

This is what was done to our sister. May she never be forgotten."

"Rojin davas, gayri slami sistemin nasl bir zulm ve hukuksuzluk rettiinin en ak kantdr.

 Beer yasalar vatandaa Allah korkusu ve iman bilinci kazandrmyor. Beer yasalara tabi insanlar yasa nnde mazbut, yasann ulaamayaca yerde olabildiince canileiyor.

 Yasa koyucular mutlak ilim sfatna sahip olmadndan su ve ceza arasnda denge kuramyor. ou 'ceza' caydrc olmak yle dursun, sua tevik ediyor.

 Yarglama srecinin uzunluu ve bu sreci reytinge kurban eden ahlaksz medya eliyle, her maduriyet magazinselleiyor. Gz yal anne ve babalar yllarca ayn acy her gn, her duruma tekrar tekrar yayor. Sistem adalet retmedii gibi adalet bekleyen madurlarn maduriyetini derinletiriyor.

 Sulu veya madur iktidar, sermaye veya sosyeteye yaknsa, sre ok hzl ve saylanlarn lehine iliyor. Aksi durumlarda sre uzuyor ve madurlarn aleyhine iliyor.

 kan karar ou zaman suun kendisinden daha fazla yaralayc oluyor. ocuu katledilen ebeveyn, vergileriyle maduru beslemek zorunda braklyor. Onlar ocuklarn kaybetmiken, sulular ya bir yarg paketi ya da kovid dzenlemesiyle dar kp hayatlarna devam ediyor. 

Sonu olarak; adliye nlerinde gz yal aresiz ebeveynler grmek istemiyorsak; adaletin Kzlay bakannn kzna ayr, Nizamettin Kabai'in kzna (Rojin) ayr ilemesini istemiyorsak, Allah'n nizamna, slam terbiyesine ve er'i yasalara dnmeliyiz."

text:
"It these quburists, who claim to be from Ahl al-Sunna, read some books they would realize that it was Allh who revived the dead after Eis (peace be upon him) prayed to Allh for it.

Imm al-abar said:

Eis (peace be upon him) revived the deceased by praying to Allh for them; thus, his prayer was answered.

| Tafsr al-abar, vol. 5, p. 424.

Even the Ashar scholar, Fakhr al-Deen al-Rz, said:

Is it permissible to say that He (Allh the Most High) placed within the self of Eis (peace be upon him) a characteristic in the sense that whenever he blew into something his blowing would necessitate for that thing to become alive? Or to say that it isnt like that; but rather, Allh, the Most High, used to create life within that body with His ability at the moment when Eis (peace be upon him) blew into it as a way of manifesting a miracle? This second explanation is the truth because Allh, the Most High, said: The One who created death and life (67:2). And it was mentioned that Ibrhm (peace be upon him) said in his debate with the king: My Lord is the One who gives life and cause death (2:258), so if this characteristic occurred for other than Him then that argument would have perished.

| al-Tafsr al-Kabr, by Fakhr al-Deen al-Rz, vol. 8, pp. 228 229.

Its essentially the same as the story mentioned in the following verse:

So We said: "Strike him (the dead man) with a piece of it (the cow)." Thus Allh brings the dead to life and shows you HisAyt(proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) so that you may understand.

| Qurn [Hilali & Khan Translation], ch. 2, v. 73.

In this verse, it is Allh who revived the dead man at the moment when he was struck by a piece of the cow. It wasnt the piece from the cow that revived him.

           "

text:
"Yine bir kemalist zihniyetin zulm!

7 yanda bir kz renci, 22 Nisanda okulun son saatinde tren iin dar arlyor. renci trene katlmak istemediini ve insan suretinin olmas nedeniyle orada bulunmak istemediini sylemitir. Devamnda bayraa ve stiklal Marna saygl olduunu ifade etmitir. Dini inanc gerei insan suretinin nnde durmamas gerektiini ve ailesinin de bu tr eylerden holanmadn sylemitir.

Bunun zerine nbeti retmen durumu erkek mdr yardmcsna iletmi ve mdr yardmcs kzn kolundan tutup zorla tren alanna karmtr. Burada mdr yardmcs birok su ilemitir:

* ocuun beden dokunulmazln ihlal etmitir
* Cebir ve zor kullanmtr
* Kii zgrln kstlamtr
* Grevini ktye kullanmtr
* ocua psikolojik bask uygulamtr

Anayasamz kendi iinde elimektedir. Din ve inan hrriyeti maddesi vardr. Bu maddenin kapsam, bir kiinin Kurana ve hadislere iman etmesi ve emir ile yasaklarna uymasdr. rnek olarak bir renci teneffste namaz klmak isterse ve retmen buna izin vermezse bu su oluturur. Ayn ekilde insan suretinin nnde sayg duruunda bulunmak inancna gre yasak ise kimse bu yasa inemesi iin zorlayamaz.

Bu sadece Mustafa Kemal iin deil, kim olursa olsun ayndr. Gerekse Fatih Sultan Mehmet olsun, yine de fikri deimeyecektir. nk bu durum siyasi deil, dini bir inancn gerekliliidir.

Suret hakknda sahih hadisler ve ayetler vardr:

* Melekler, iinde kpek ve suret bulunan eve girmez. (Buhr, Mslim)
* Kyamet gn en iddetli azaba urayacak olanlar, suret yapanlardr. (Buhr, Mslim)
* Bu suretleri yapanlara kyamet gn azap edilecek ve Yarattklarnza can verin denilecektir. (Buhr, Mslim)

Objektif olmak gerekirse, kimisine gre lkeyi o kurtard, cumhuriyeti o kurdu; kimisine gre sarayn emriyle Samsuna kt, cumhuriyeti kurarken meclisi aniden toplayp dier vekillerin meclise gelmesini engelledi vs. Bu tartmalar bir kenara brakarak unu sylemek istiyorum:

Daha birka gn nce kck rencilerin arafn kt olduu ve mini etein uygar olduu el kadar ocuklara retilmeye alldn grdk. Kadnlara zgrlk bizim zamanmzda verildi diyerek rtl kadnlarn retmen olma zgrln acmaszca alabildiniz. Kurtulu savanda Mustafa Kemalin arkasnda Arap askerler vard grmediniz. Kurtulu savanda Krt kardelerimiz vard, sava sonrasnda bombalar Krtlere attnz. Kurtulu savanda cepheye yardm gtren arafl annelerimiz vard, imdi ayplyorsunuz.

Alkol alp, LGBTyi savunup, mini etek giymeyi uygarlk sanyorsunuz ok yazk. 

Yeniden dirili balad  !"

text:
Upon the authority of Abu Hurairah, the Messenger of Allh (sallallhu 'alaihi wa sallam) said: 

When the month of Ramadan enters, the doors of Paradise are opened, the doors of Hellfire are closed, and the (chief) devils are chained.
[Bukhari, Muslim]

Shaykh Ibn Baz (May Allh have mercy on him) mentioned in explanation of the above hadith:

"This indicates the virtue of this great month and that Allah, Glorified and Exalted, grants His servants success in performing many righteous deeds during it, deeds for which the doors of Paradise are opened and the doors of the heavens are opened, as occurs in another narration: The doors of the heavens are opened.

In this is an encouragement to increase in acts of goodness such as prayer, giving charity, saying Subhnallh, saying L ilha illallh, and other than that to seize this opportunity; the opportunity of this great month and what it contains of Allahs generosity, His kindness, and the vastness of His benevolence.

Likewise, (seizing the opportunity found in) the chaining of the (chief) devils and their inability to reach what they used to reach (of evil) in other months, and that their evil is weakened."

 Explanation of Shaykh ibn Baz on Riyd As-Slihn (2nd explanation, no. 389) - taken from the Shaykh's website.

text:
" AL MARKAZ AS SALAFI LEICESTERSHIRE FUNDRAISING APPEAL

Help Establish a Salafi Markaz (Centre) & Place of Worship in Leicester! 

 Donation Details at the bottom of the Tweet.

 Our Goal

Alhamdulillah, the Salafi dawah has a long history in the City of Leicester, with lessons and gatherings being held for over two decades. Many people in the community have greatly benefited from these durs & halaqt and the opportunity to study the Quran and Authentic Sunnah upon the understanding of the Sahabah & Salaf (righteous predecessors).

By the permission of Allah, a venue has now been earmarked where a dedicated Salafi Markaz and place of worship can be established.

We are now seeking the support of the community to help us secure and prepare this space so that it can serve as a centre for prayer, learning, and dawah.

 What This Markaz Will Be Used For?

The aim is to establish a simple and beneficial space that will be used for:

 Regular Islamic lessons and Study Circles
 Propagating the Quran and Authentic Sunnah
 Community Gatherings for beneficial knowledge & Tawun upon Birr & Taqwa
 A place where Muslims can learn their Religion correctly
 Working towards Establishing the 5 daily prayers in Congregation

This Markaz will, in sh Allh, serve as a place of worship and learning for the local community.

 Why Your Support Matters?

Establishing a place where people can gather to learn their religion and remember Allah brings ongoing benefit to the community in this life & the next.

The Messenger of Allah  said:
When a person dies, his deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who supplicates for him. (Sahih Muslim)

Helping establish a hub of ilm (knowledge) & ibdah (worship) can be a form of ongoing charity (Sadaqah Jriyah).

 How You Can Help

If you are able, you can support this effort by:

- Making a donation
- Sharing this fundraiser with others
- Making du that Allah allows this Markaz to be established

 HOW TO DONATE 

 GoFundMe:


 PayPal:


 Bank Transfer:
Name: Clear Guidance Ltd
Sort Code: 40-28-06
Account Number: 45387531

International Transfer
IBAN: GB91HBUK40280645387531
BIC/SWIFT: HBUKGB4B or HBUKGB4BXXX

May Allh reward everyone who supports this effort and place Barakah in it.",

I wished Islam to continue winning and to achieve his biggest dreams, both in the ring and in life.""
"

These are all high IQ points and contradictions that only I, a person well versed in this history, can point out. Nobody else does. Nobody else calls them out on it."

text:
"Shaykh li al-Fawzn speaks about those who allow the disbelievers into the lands of muslims when assisting them against the muslims and how this is disbelief even if he hates their religion.

Interestingly, he then speaks about those who bring them in and enabled them to build churches and the likes, and this is what he says :

_

Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al Wahhb said: [That if someone openly displays their] agreement with their religion [of the Mushrikin]

Shaykh li al-Fawzn said: Meaning: He enabled them [the mushrikin] to establish churches in the lands of the Muslims, and granted them freedom, and said to the Muslims: Do not prevent them from anything; whatever they request from you, comply with it. This is mudhanah, because our religion forbids this.



Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb said: [And if he does this then] indeed he is a disbeliever like them.

Shaykh li al-Fawzn commentates: Because he enabled the [religion of the] disbelievers and was pleased with their religion, and he forewent what the religion of Islm obliged upon him  namely disassociating the religion of the disbelievers and disavowing it. He did not disavow it; rather, he permitted it, justified it, and granted them freedom [to establish their religion] in the lands of the Muslims.

Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb said: [And if he does this then indeed he is a disbeliever like them] even if he detests their religion and hates them.

Shaykh li al-Fawzn commentates: Meaning: if he loves their religion, then this makes him a disbeliever (kfir), an apostate, if he establish them. And if he establishes them while he hates their religion, then this act of his constitutes apostasy from Islm, because he has assisted them against the Muslims.

        (31,32)",

"Ash-Shaykh Bad'ud-dn Ar-Rshid, Allah have mercy on him, authored Bad'ut-Tafsr, a tafsr in the Sindhi language. He mentions some of the reasons for this:

"Out region of Sindh (Pakistan), the majority of it is void of knowledge and knowing Allah the Most High. It is for this reason that blind allegiance to madh-habs and following (sufi) orders has dominated the area."

(Muqaddimatu-Tafsril-Qurn-al-Karm, P.59)

Benefit: If we want to improve ourselves and our standing in front of Allah in the hereafter, then we have to correct our sajdah in front of Him in this life. This can only be accomplished with knowledge.

Allah have mercy upon him."

text:
"Shaykh Muhammad in alih al-'Uthaymin said:

"Certainly, respecting the scholars and the leaders is obligatory because religious legislation is upheld by doing so. The scholars are the carriers of the legislation and if they are not respected [as individuals], then neither will their sayings be respected. 

The leaders are the protectors of the legislations - meaning in regards to its application upon the Ummah, so if they are not respected, then this - i.e. implementation of the legislation, will be lost, and chaos will ensue. "

- Ibn al-'Uthaymin, Muhammad ibn alih, Wasayat wa at-Tawjiht li ulb al-Ilm (p.404)."

text:
" This is Why anaf Scholars Took So Much Caution with Takfr (Obv other Madhabs too)

1 Mull Al al-Qr al-anaf   (d. 1014 AH) said:

The scholars have stated regarding the matter of declaring someone a disbeliever (takfr): if there are 99 possible interpretations indicating kufr (disbelief) and only one that is not kufr, it is better for the muft and the judge to adopt the interpretation that is not kufr.

He further explained that this principle is derived from the saying of the Prophet :

Avert punishments from the Muslims as much as you can. If you find a way out for a Muslim, then let him go.

 [Shar al-Shif al-Q Iy, 2/502]

2 Imm Ibn bidn al-anaf   (d. 1252 AH) said:

The act of disbelief is a grave matter, so I do not declare a believer as a disbeliever. Whenever I find a narration stating [kufr], he is not to be declared as a kfir.

In summary: If there are aspects in an issue that necessitate declaring takfr [excommunication] and one aspect prevents him [from committing kufr], then the muft should lean towards the aspect that prevents declaring takfr on the Muslim, in order to maintain a good opinion towards the Muslim.

Additionally, in al-Bazzyyah, it is added: Except if one explicitly states an intention affirming kufr [disbelief], interpretation [which protected him from takfr] does not benefit them.

 [Radd al-Mutr al al-Durr al-Mukhtr, 4/408]"

text:
""Wahhabism is the tool of extremism and modern day terrorism. 

It began as theology fused with power.

Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab was an 18th-century cleric from Najd who preached uncompromising monotheism and condemned what he saw as religious deviation. But his defining weapon was not spirituality. It was takfir: declaring other Muslims outside the fold.

And even inside his own home, there was alarm.

His father, Abd al-Wahhab ibn Sulayman, reportedly warned people not to follow his sons extremism. His own brother, Sulayman ibn Abd al-Wahhab, wrote an entire refutation:

      
Al-Sawaiq al-Ilahiyya fi al-Radd ala al-Wahhabiyya
(Divine Thunderbolts in Refutation of the Wahhabis)

In it, he warned that reckless takfir leads to bloodshed. He compared the movement to early Kharijite extremism  religion weaponized against Muslims. And, that is exactly what happened in the following years.

Then came 1744.

The pact between Ibn Abd al-Wahhab and Muhammad bin Saud in Diriyah.

The formula was simple and ruthless:

Al-Sheikh holds religion.
Al-Saud holds power.

Through that alliance, theology became state machinery.

Expansion was framed as purification. Campaigns like the attack on Karbala in 1802 are documented in multiple chronicles. Sectarian violence was justified as religious correction. Unity meant obedience. Dissent meant deviance.

That fusion : Religion as political legitimacy became the backbone of the Saudi state model.

Fast forward to the 20th century.

Oil wealth globalized the doctrine. Religious institutions expanded. Clerical authority was centralized. Universities such as Imam Muhammad ibn Saud Islamic University became exporters of a rigid interpretation of Salafism.

Then came Afghanistan.

During the Soviet war, Saudi religious rhetoric aligned with U.S. anti-communist strategy. Arab Afghans were mobilized under religious banners. The ideological infrastructure created centuries earlier proved adaptable to modern geopolitics.

From that environment emerged figures like Osama bin Laden, founder of Al-Qaeda. Later movements from Al-Qaeda to ISIS drew heavily from Takfiri Wahhabi logic: excommunication as a political weapon to justify jihad and murder. 

This does not mean Ibn Abd al-Wahhab sat in the 18th century planning 9/11.

It means that when you normalize declaring Muslims apostates, and when state power protects that logic, you build an intellectual ecosystem extremists can later radicalize further.

The 9/11 hijackers, European terror cells, sectarian militias in Iraq and Syria  they justified violence using theological frameworks built on takfir and militant purification.

That ideological pipeline matters.

Throughout modern Middle Eastern politics, religion was repeatedly used as leverage:

Against Arab nationalism.
Against Jamal Abdulnasser.
During Cold War alignments.
During regional rivalries.

Each time, theology served strategy.

And now?

When Saudi enters political conflict whether in Yemen, Qatar, or against the UAE  the instinctive tool is familiar: religious framing. Accuse. Label. Delegitimize.

Same method. New arena.

History shows the pattern:

When religion becomes state armor, power hides behind piety.
When dissent becomes heresy, control becomes sacred.
And when takfir becomes political currency, violence follows.

The earliest warning came from inside the family itself. But were silenced so Bin Saud and Alsheikh can carry out their gruesome acts of genocide against the Shia people in Alhassa and then in 1802, Saudi-Wahhabi forces attacked Karbala and killed under the justification of Takfir."
"
--------------

Zio-Emiratism is a tool of 

The reality, as i stated before, is that many of these  funded Emiratis hate Islam and Tawheed

May Allah have mercy on Shaykh Muhammad Ibn Abdulwahhab

He is free from the lies you associate with him,

text:
Shaykh ammd Al-Anr (may Allh have mercy on him) said:

When I arrived at a place near Makkah, I met some Bedouins and asked their leader: What are the pillars of Islam?

He replied, Ash-Shfi, Ab anfah, Mlik  and I forgot the fourth one!

So I said to those around him, Remind him  mention the fourth.

They said, We have forgotten.

I then said to them, Amad (ibn anbal).

They all exclaimed together, Yes, thats the one!

He added:

This happened when we went out for dawah in the year 1375 AH.

[Collected in his biography]

text:
"A Warning To Those That Defend Themselves But Are Silent When The Truth & It's People Are Violated 

Ibn Qayyim    said: 

"And what religion, and what good, is there in a person who sees that which Allah has prohibited being violated, His boundaries being ignored and transgressed upon, His religion being abandoned, and the Sunnah of His Messenger being rejected? Yet his heart is unmoved, and his tongue remains silent. Indeed, he is a mute devil, just like one who speaks with falsehood is an articulate devil. 

Has the religion (of Allah) ever been tested with anything other than the likes of these people? Those who, so long as their food and (positions of) leadership are secured and protected, have no concern for what happens to the religion of Allah. The best among them may feel slight sadness, but should he be challenged regarding something he holds dearwhether concerning his status or his wealthhe will strive and toil to the utmost of his ability, using the three levels of rejection and rebuke to defend himself, (i.e. they use their hands, their tongues, and their heart to; change, repel, reject, and rebuke that evil). 

These individuals, though they have fallen in the sight of Allah and are under His anger, have been tested in this worldly life with the greatest of trials, yet they do not feel it. Certainly, for sure, it is the death of the hearts. For indeed, the more alive the heart is, the stronger its anger is for the sake of Allah and His Messenger, and the more complete its defence of the religion shall be."

['Ilm al-Muwaqq'n 'an Rabb al-'lamn Ibn al-Qayyim 2/177] translated by Abu Ukkashah Abdulhakeem"

Imm as-Sijz (d.444Ah) said:

So whoever seeks salvation from such [innovators], and safety from the innovations, must make the Qurn and the narrations his scale in everything he hears and sees. If he is knowledgeable of them, he should judge all matters by them and by adherence to the way of the Salaf.

And let him not accept any statement from anyone unless he demands from him, in proof of its correctness, either:

1) a clear, decisive Ayah, or 
2) an established Sunnah, or 
3) the statement of a Sahab with a sound chain.

text:
"The school of dilution and adaptation 

Question:
What do you mean by the dilution (tamyee) and adaptation (tatwee) that you mentioned?

Shaykh Abu Yahya al-Libi (may Allah have mercy upon him) said:

This approach is one of the afflictions that has befallen our Islamic Ummah in this age, and many have been tested by it, those described as thinkers, enlightened individuals, moderates, or centrists.

The reality of this methodology is the dilution of the truths of the deen and their reshaping and subjugation so that they conform to understandings, perspectives, and convictions that its proponents have adopted, especially in matters where they feel there is conflict or tension with ideas promoted by Westerners.

So you find these adapters sparing no effort and leaving no means unused to try to prove that Islam agrees with Western ideas, matches their rulings, or even preceded them in what they have proposed.

Unfortunately, this contagion has affected many Muslim scholars and preachers, to the point that some of them have become leaders of this school, those scholars upon whom Allah placed the responsibility of conveying His message to people as it is, without distortion or deception, and who should not be swayed by desire or fear.

As Allah the Exalted said:

Those who convey the messages of Allah and fear Him, and do not fear anyone except Allah. And sufficient is Allah as a Reckoner.

This school of dilution and adaptation has made all matters of the deen into an open domain, freely entered by their intellects, polluted by their ideas, and subjected to their research, without any restraint or trustworthiness holding them back.

This is a great calamity of our time.

The Quran has already clarified this reality and exposed the inner motives of such people, namely deviation and corruption of the heart, which can be summarized as infatuation with Western culture, and being carried away by intellectual temptation.

Allah says:

As for those in whose hearts is deviation, they follow what is ambiguous, seeking Fitna and seeking its interpretation.

The Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) recited this verse and then said:

If you see those who follow what is ambiguous, they are the ones Allah has named, so beware of them.

Therefore, a Muslim, so that his deen remains sound and he protects himself from deviation and getting lost in paths of misguidance, must beware of such people and not be deceived by names, titles, or fame.

What they are doing is polluting our Islamic sources and corrupting our pure concepts, which Islam was keen to preserve in their independence, in terminology, meanings, and substance, so that no impurity would enter and disturb this pure spring.

In this regard, there is the incident when the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) was approached by Umar (may Allah be pleased with him), who said:
We hear statements from the Jews that we find appealing, should we write some of them down?

The Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) replied:

Are you becoming confused as the Jews and Christians became confused? I have brought it to you pure and clear. If Musa were alive, he would have no choice but to follow me.

Today, you see that whenever a Western thinker praises something from Islam, it is taken as proof of the truth of the message, as though we are in need of such endorsements.
Even worse, and more dangerous, is that the situation has reversed, these people of such thinking now reshape Islamic meanings so that they serve as evidence for the correctness of many aspects of Western culture, even when that culture fundamentally contradicts Islam in both concepts and terminology.

I believe we are in need of a thorough study of the foundations and principles of this deviant and manipulative school, in order to uproot it and expose its danger to the core principles of the religion, the extent to which it corrupts its concepts, ......

Continued  "

text:
"So we rejected this statement and said: this is a dangerous statement. Its implication is that you are nullifying the Fatw of the scholars in Nawzil. If you see that there is a mistake, and you are a Salaf who respects the scholars and also respects the rulers, then write to them. Present your viewpoint only. But to establish this as a general principlethis is false and wrong! 

This resembles the approach of the Surrs and Qubs during the Gulf crisis, who said: Our scholars do not understand current affairswe understand current affairs. America entered, Europe did such-and-such, and you, O scholarslike ibn Bzyou are only with the rulers, obeying them. The same wordsonly the people and circumstances have changed. There it was a Gulf crisis related to the entry of disbelievers (to the lands of the Muslims); here it is a COVID crisis related to medicine.

Furthermore, this man (making these claims) works in medicine. If we look at his specialization, do you work in every field of medicine and every specialty? No. Then speak only within your level. That is one point.

Secondly, do you want the entire Ummah to abandon the doctors of the world and the consultants, and instead follow your opinion? This is dangerous speech! It is unclear what this person intends with such statements. 

Despite that, he has been advised, and we have advised him before. We even sent to him years ago when he issued a Fatw. Look at this Fatwmany people may not even be aware of it. He issued a ruling that social distancing was Minor Shirk. That means Al-Fawzn has fallen into Shirk, the Muft has fallen into Shirk, and our scholars such as Ubayd and others have fallen into Shirk. And we, the students of knowledge, have all fallen into Shirk. And the Fatw of the scholars who permitted distancing are, according to him, Fatw of Shirk.

So who is behind you that you speak with such words? What do you want, that you strike at the scholars by claiming they have fallen into (minor) Shirk, that they should not be referred to, and that they neither understand nor comprehend? This is what he says in the audio recordings. 

Some have not even listened to these recordings yet and do not want to listenand this is a calamity. You find a person plunging headlong into Fitnah while knowing nothing. And if you say to him, Come, let us sit and clarify things for you, he refuses to listen. This is blind partisanship. 

As for us, we clarify the error. Whoever is guided, then all praise is due to Allh, and whoever goes astray, then it is upon himself.

Shaykh Abdul-Azz ibn Bz was asked on his official site: Should we refer only to doctors, or to the scholars? He replied: No. One must return to the scholar. If the matter were purely medical, then yeswe would return to the doctors; because they are the people of specialization. But this concerns a matter connected to medicine and also to the Sharah.

Does Al-Fawzn immediately issue a ruling when a purely medical issue comes to him? You think highly of yourself and consider yourself an expert. But when this scholarAl-Fawzn, the Muft, Al-Abbd, Rab, and othersare asked about a worldly technical matter such as medicine or agriculture, do they refer to specialists or not? They do refer to the specialists. This shows that you do not understand how the scholars issue Fatw.

I have already explained this in detail in one of the refutationsthe methodology of the scholar in giving Fatwand it is published on my channel. Yet some people ignore all of this and do not want to listen. 

Continued"

He is to be called to repent; if he repents, well and good, otherwise his neck is to be struck, and he is to be thrown onto some of the rubbish heaps so that neither the Muslims nor the dhimms are harmed by the putrid stench of his corpse. "As Muslims, we must stand firm upon our faith and reject those who falsely claim to represent us. Iran, often portrayed as a defender of Muslim causes, cannot and must not be supported, because:

1 Their belief system has no connection to Islam. Their aqdah strays far from Tawd and slips into shirk, by elevating their Imams to near-divine status and seeking intercession through them in ways that clearly contradict the oneness of Allah. Their deep rooted hatred towards the Prophet Muammads (peace be upon him) noble companions and honorable wives, especially ishah (may Allah be Pleased with her), is a major red line that no sincere Muslim can ignore.

2 Iran has the blood of countless Muslims on its hands. In fact, its crimes in places like Syria and Yemen have caused more death and destruction than even Israel.

 Our stance is not to support either side. Rather, their internal conflict weakens their grip on the region while keeping them preoccupied with themselves. This may give our brothers and sisters in Gaza and elsewhere some breathing space and relief.

Some ask: Why not support Iran, when the so-called Sunni Arab countries are doing nothing for Gaza?
The truth is, even these regimes have nothing to do with Islam. They are secular puppets of America and Israel. The ordinary people (awm) in these nations are either powerless, or live in fear, trapped behind blocked nationalistic borders. Their rulers serve foreign interests. Not the Ummah.

Had these countries been ruled by sincere Muslims, united in Tawd and loyalty to Allah, the Zionist entity Israel would have collapsed long ago. Instead, they remain idle while Gaza bleeds proving their betrayal beyond any doubt.

As Muslims, we must reject both Irans deviant sectarian agenda and the treachery of pro-Western regimes.",

text:
""Tonight is the night most hoped to be Laylat al-Qadr.
Do not waste this night. Do not indulge in arm.
Stand before Allah in your alh and prolong your standing before Him. Increase in dhikr, alh, du, and the recitation of the Qurn.
Make du and pour your heart out to Allah. Do not hesitate for even a second.
May Allah grant us and you success."
"
--------------

Ramadhan 1447 (Day 27) 
The Night Most Hoped to Be Laylat Al-Qadr 

This is the view held by the majority of scholars, and it is considered the strongest and most well-known opinion. The evidence includes the following hadiths:

1 The Prophet  said:
((      ))
If someone is searching for it, let him look for it on the twenty-seventh night. [Sahih al-Jami, no. 2920] 

2 Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) reported that a man came to the Prophet  and said: O Messenger of Allah, I am an old and weak man, and standing in prayer at night is difficult for me. Tell me a night in which Allah may allow me to reach Laylat Al-Qadr. The Prophet  said:
(( ))
Focus on the seventh night (meaning the 27th). [Authenticated by Ibn Rajab in Lataif Al-Maarif and by Al-Arnaut in his checking of Musnad Ahmad] 

3 Muawiyah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah  said:
((    ))
Laylat Al-Qadr is the twenty-seventh night. [Sahih Abi Dawud, no. 1386] 

4 Ubayy ibn Kab (may Allah be pleased with him) was certainhe even sworethat it was the 27th night. When he was asked how he knew this, he said: By the sign the Messenger of Allah  told us: the sun rises the next morning without strong rays. [Reported by Muslim, no. 762] 

5 The Prophet  led the companions in night prayer during some nights until one-third of the night had passed, and on some nights until half the night. But on the 27th night, he led them in prayer until morning. [Authenticated by al-Albani in As-Silsilah 7/101] 

6 On the 27th night, the Prophet  gathered his family and wives (unlike the 23rd and 25th nights) and prayed with them until they feared they might miss the pre-dawn meal (suhoor).
[Sahih Ibn Majah, no. 1100] 

 The 27th night begins at sunset of the 26th day of Ramadan.

We ask Allah to allow us and all Muslims to reach it and to be among those who stand in prayer during it with faith and sincere hope for reward.

Source: 

#_
#Ramadan@masjidsahabah

""A short afternoon nap restores brain neuroplasticity.

Take the midday rest (qayllah), for the devils do not take a midday rest.

And from the Salaf, Khawwt ibn Jubayr    said:

Sleeping at the beginning of the day is harmful, in the middle of it is restorative, and at its end is foolishness.



Once again modern science is catching up to what the Prophet  guided us to over 1400 years ago. 

As Muslims we need to understand that the Sunnah isnt just spiritual guidance that happens to agree with science sometimes; it is advice from our Creator who knows best how our bodies and brains function optimally.

This is exactly why studying the Prophets  guidance on health is so important; its something to be learned and actually implemented in daily life. If we had the same level of reliance upon that guidance as we do on modern medicine, we wouldnt need to look elsewhere.

Shaykh Muhammad ibn 'Abdullatf l Ash-Shaykh said:

"Allh - blessed and exalted be He - deals with His servants according to how they place Him as it relates to themselves.

Whoever reveres the command of Allh, obeys Him, avoids His prohibitions, and fears Him in secret and in public, Allh will be pleased with him and grant him happiness.

But whomsoever opposes His commands and embarks upon His prohibitions, and gives precedence to his desires over obedience to his Master, Allh will take retribution against him and distance him. As you act, so shall you be treated; an appropriate recompense. And your Lord is not at all unjust to (His) slaves."

 Durar as-Siniyyah fil Ajwibah an-Najdiah (14/289)"

text:
" Generosity in the Month of Raman

 Sh Abu Fuayl Abdur Raqb ( ).

 

 - Seeking nearness to Allh by way of giving in charity and being plentiful in generosity in this blessed month of Raman.
- Believe in Allh and His Messenger and spend out of that in which He has made you successors. For those who have believed among you and spent, there will be a great reward. [Srah al-Hadd 57:7]
- So fear Allh as much as you are able and listen and obey and spend [in the way of Allh]; it is better for your selves. And whoever is protected from the stinginess of his soul - it is those who will be the successful. [Srah at-Taghbun 64:16]
- Small deeds that will benefit us in the hereafter and protect us from the punishment of Allh.
- How Allh cultivates charity given from lawful earnings.
- So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it. And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it. [Srah az-Zalzalah 99:7-8]
- The meaning of .
- A adth that highlights the outcome of a deed that emanates from fear of Allh, mercy, love or sincerity, even if it is small.
- Focusing on recitation and implementation of the Qurn in Raman.
- The reason for the Messengers  increased generosity in the month of Raman.
- Fruits of reciting and reflecting upon the Qurn.
- The Messengers  detachment from the duny.",

text:
"The misconception of excuse of ignorance in major shirk did not arise before the time of Ibn Taymiyyah. Those who argue for excusing ignorance in acts of shirk mostly cite his statements as their earliest basis.

It first surfaced during the era of Shaykh Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab ( ) in two forms:
1.From clear deviants, whom the Shaykh refuted in Mufd al-Mustafd.
2.From some sincere but confused individuals, including certain students in Diriyyah and al-Ahsa. The doubt subsided after.

It later re-emerged in the next generation during the time of Abd al-Rahman ibn Hasan, adopted by Dawud ibn Jirjis and Uthman ibn Mansur. Shaykh Abd al-Rahman countered it, supported by his son Abd al-Latif and Shaykh Aba Butayn through well-known writings.

In the third generation, it reappeared once more and was refuted by Shaykh Ishaq ibn Abd al-Rahman and Shaykh Ibn Sahman via fatawa and writings, with support from Shaykh Abd al-Latifs sons, Abdullah and Ibrahim.

(  Excerpt from           )"

"Standard Madkhali idiocy exposed by their lacking of basic reading comprehension, as well as doing us a favor by using as proof the quote (likely copied and pasted from another Madkhali Zindq) that refutes him. So those people, if they know that it is not permissible for them to judge except by what Allah has revealed (here Ibn Taymiyyah establishes they dont do creedal Istihalal), yet they do not adhere to that (here Ibn Taymiyyah distinguishes between creedal belief, and not outwardly adhering to something); rather, they deem it halal (linguistically, i.e., Istihlal Amali) to judge contrary to what Allah has revealed  then they are disbelievers.

Istihlal Amali by consensus of Ahlul Sunnah isnt Kufr or Shirk, unless the action itself is inherently Kufr or Shirk. If this is merely about linguistic Istihlal to govern by man made laws, what do you think it is with legislating man made laws yourself? Did Ibn Taymiyyah make Tawbah from this Fatwa before he died? Keep in mind, Ibn Taymiyyah starts with There is no doubt, and this is a point of consensus amongst the classical Muslims. 

This moron Madkhali highlights the latter Istihlal, thinking its creedal, when the sentence before it explicitly negates that. This more or less, summaries how Madkhalis are with Daleel.

This is why, 99% of every Madkhali Shubuha can be refuted with simply possessing basic Arabic reading comprehension."

text:
Israeli Forces Tortured Child in Front of His Father in Gaza  

Israeli forces subjected a one-year-old child to abuse and torture to pressure his father into giving confessions in Al-Maghazi refugee camp in central Gaza, according to local and family sources.

Osama Abu Nassar, while taking his child to buy supplies a few days ago, he was caught in gunfire near his home and forced by Israeli soldiers to leave his 18-month-old son on the ground and approach the Israeli checkpoint, where he was stripped of his clothes.

According to witnesses, the army then took the child and interrogated the father at the checkpoint. The forces tortured the child in front of his father, including burning his leg with cigarettes, pricking, and inserting a metal nail into his leg, as confirmed by a medical report.

The child, Karim, was released after 10 hours and handed over to his family through the International Red Cross, while the father remains in detention.

 Osama Al-Kahlout

text:
"Wallahi an unforgettable journey! From Kuwait to Makkah, it was truly beautiful! and that is the same sentiment anyone who attended has. The many sittings, whether with the people of knowledge or the students of knowledge or just the many brotherly sittings over good food or coffee, the whole occasion was monumental and hugely beneficial for the soul!

It brings to mind the statement of Imm Ibnil Qayyim:

Keeping the company of the righteous transforms you from six states to six others:
1.From doubt to certainty.
2.From showing off (riy) to sincerity.
3.From heedlessness to remembrance (dhikr).
4.From desiring the worldly life to desiring the Hereafter.
5.From arrogance to humility.
6.From bad intention to genuine sincere naah.

(Ighthat al-Lahfn 1/136)

May Allah return us for many more!",

text:
"What is the Nisb of cash money today? How much Zakt do you pay?

Question: How much is a gram of silver today?

Answer: 2.03

Question: What is the Nisb for silver upon which Zakt is paid?

Answer: 595 grams (or above)

Question: What is the Nisb for paper money or money saved in banks?

Answer: Same as the Nisb of silver  i.e., 595 grams (silver).

Question: So, what does that come to today?

Answer: 
It is 595 grams x 2.03 = 1,207.85 GBP
(Which is $1,620.03 USD and 1,394.53 EUR)

If you have this amount or aboveand you've had it for a yearyou must pay Zakt upon it.

Conclusion: If you have this amount of cash [or more] in your possession for a year, you are obligated to pay 2.5% on all of it  as Zakt.

Quick Example:
You have 5,000 in total savings (that you've had for a year): Therefore, you must pay 2.5% of that as your annual Zakt, which comes to 125.

Abu Khadeejah.

Pay online to our UK based registered charity:,

There are many violations during Eid that many people fall into. We will mention some of them so that we can be wary of them and avoid falling into them. These include:
1. Adorning oneself by shaving the beard: Know, my brother, that shaving the beard is forbidden (Haram) and a sin. The evidence for this is the hadith of Ibn Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (Trim the mustaches and let the beards grow.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5393).
And from Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (Trim the mustaches, and let the beards grow long; differ from the Magians.)  Narrated by Muslim (260).
Sheikh Ibn Baz, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And this wording in the mentioned hadiths necessitates the obligation of growing and lengthening the beards, and the prohibition of shaving and cutting them, because the default principle regarding commands is obligation, and the default principle regarding prohibitions is prohibition, unless there is evidence indicating otherwise, and this is what is relied upon by the people of knowledge.)  Majmoo Al-Fatawa (3/372).
> 
2. Dyeing the hair black: Know, my brother, that it is not permissible to dye the hair black, and a severe threat has been reported for those who do so. From Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with them both, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (There will be a people at the end of time who will dye their hair black like the crops of pigeons; they will not smell the fragrance of Paradise.)  Narrated by Ahmad (2470), and Abu Dawud (4212).
> (So this hadith is explicit regarding the prohibition of dyeing with black.)  Tuhfat Al-Ahwadhi (5/359).
> 
Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And this hadith necessitates the prohibition of dyeing gray hair black, and that it is from the major sins.)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (11/123).
> 
3. Isbal (Lengthening the garment below the ankles): Isbal of the lower garment is one of the major sins because a severe threat is attached to it, and the one who practices Isbal has fallen into two prohibitions:
 * The First: He has exposed himself to punishment. From Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, who said: (Whatever of the lower garment is below the ankles is in the Fire.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5787).
 * The Second: He has exposed his garment to dirt and filth. A young man wearing a long garment entered upon Umar bin Al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, and he said to him: (O son of my brother, raise your garment, for it is cleaner for your garment, and more pious toward your Lord.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari in the story of the assassination of Umar (3700).
The Sunnah is for the lower garment and clothing of the Muslim to be at mid-calf. From Al-Alaa bin Abdul Rahman, from his father, that he said: I asked Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri about the lower garment, and he said: I will inform you with knowledge; I heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, say: (The lower garment of the believer is to the middle of his calves. There is no sin on him for what is between that and the ankles. Whatever is lower than that is in the Fire, whatever is lower than that is in the Fire. Allah will not look on the Day of Resurrection at the one who drags his lower garment out of arrogance.)  Narrated by Malik in Al-Muwatta (2/914).
So the Muslim must beware of Isbal.

text:
A tremendous evening with our brothers and sisters in London at @_MuSunnah in Alperton (Thursday 9th April 2026). 

Msh-Allh, the Markaz was filled with listeners. With barely a days notice, Ahlus-Sunnah travelled from all across London and beyond to join us. The evening began with a dars by Shaikh @uwaysT followed by Shaikh @AbuIdrees and finally a reminder from myself. 

It is important that we learn and convey this Deen with sincerity and good manners, becoming good examples for those around usthat we are humble and distant from the corrupting influences of social mediathat we take the Messenger Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) as our example, and then his Companions (Allah be pleased with them)that we love those those who love and follow them, for they are Ahlul-Hadth was-Sunnahand thereafter, the foremost among them in every age are the scholars (may Allah raise their rank). I can say that over the years, we have benefited greatly from the noble manners of the ulamshaping the way we behave and deal with the peopleand I seek pardon from Allah for my shortcomings.

So, in the West, our communities should focus on seeking knowledge; then act on what we learn, cultivate our children, nurture righteousness in our homes and hold fast to the Sunnah. 

Furthermore, Hijrah should be an important discussion in our households: actively planning and searching for a home in a Muslim country where there are already Salafi communities, however small. 

Know that Hijrah from non-Muslim lands to the lands of Islam is an obligationand a protection for you, your family and children; a safety net against the abandonment of religion today and in generations to come, insh-Allh. 

I also advise that you should not waste your time (and wealth) following the fashions, sinful habits and clothing of the non-Muslims because Islam has its own unique and proud heritage and identitynor should you follow and obsess over the modern-day offerings of entertainment (such as football, boxing and movies) that are designed to divert people away from the realities of life and its purpose: the worship of Allah and following the Messenger (peace and blessings be upon him). Focus, O Muslim, on what benefits you in this life and the Hereafter, brakallhu feekum. 

Do not be impressed by social media influencers who seek only views, likes and followers. Our example is the Prophet of Islamwho taught us to be humble, truthful and sincerethen his Companions and the Righteous Salaf. In these times you see that example reflected in the manners of the scholars and their conduct (may Allah preserve the living and have mercy upon the deceased). 

So, I advise you to get off your mobile devices and drastically limit viewing social media platformsinstead read books, attend duroos, get married, have children, focus on your families and build true Islamic homes. 

May Allah aid the Muslims and guide them to the Sunnah and the path of Ahlul-Hadthand that is the Madhhab of the Salaf. 

In thisby Allahthere is honour, just as Umar (Allah be pleased with him) said: We were a lowly people, and Allah honoured us with Islam. So if we seek honour in other than Islam, then Allah will make us lowly.

Abu Khadeejah.

" Last Ten Days  Ponder How Quickly The Month Has Passed, Likewise, Your Life Will Pass Quickly 

Sh Abu Khadeejah Abdul-Wid (may Allh preserve him). @AbuKhadeejahSP | @MasjidSalafi 

 

  Pondering over how quickly this life passes us by.
 Taking advantage of the opportunities Allh has given us to make the standing easier on Yawmul-Qiymah.
 The reward for those who performed righteous deeds and didnt waste the opportunities Allh gave them.
 The dreadful outcome of those who wasted their lives and did not put forth anything for themselves.
 An encouragement for the youth to seek protection from the punishment of Allh and a warning against wasting their lives and taking evil ones as role models.
 The striving of the Messenger  in the last ten days of Raman.
 A warning against taking these last days as days of frivolity.
 Allhs mention of itikf in His Book and a brief overview of some affairs regarding it.
 The excellence of Laylatul-Qadr and an encouragement to leave matters of the duny and seek it out.
 The reward for standing in prayer with the imm on Laylatul-Qadr and the du that should be said.
 Ending Raman upon goodness.",

"The awght of the UAE
To the Yahd, they bow and extend allegiance through the Abraham Accords, a modern veil for wal to the Yahd and global powers.

As the poet once said:
    
   
   
   
How many serve the throne,
masked as masters, yet chained as slaves?
Dogs before enemy,
fierce as wolves against their own.

These events reveal not the rulers themselves, their servitude to the enemies of Islam has long been clear. Rather, they expose the shuykh who defend them, legitimize them, and the deluded followers who anoint such tyrants as legitimate rulers, labeling all who resist them as khawrij.

No coalition of 89 nations rises; no outrage is voiced; no terror labels are issued. Silence becomes policy. Indifference becomes law.

May Allah protect our brothers and sisters in Al-Fashir, shield them from harm, grant them patience, and preserve their faith; and may He hold accountable those who oppress them or aid their oppressors.

Ahmad Musa Jibril
7 Jumd all1447H"

text:
"Jam'at Ahl as-Sunnah lid-Da'wah wa'l-Jihd (JASDJ), mostly known as Boko Haram, recently released a video threatening ISWAP. The group in the video appears to be based in the Lake Chad area under the authority of the JAS overall leader Bakura Doro, aka Abu Umaymah.    

The speaker begins by calling ISWAP apostates and deviants, and tells them not to rejoice over the death of Abu Bakr al-Shekawi (Shekau). As a reminder, Shekau blew himself up in 2021 when he was cornered by ISWAP in the Sambisa Forest. ISWAP had called upon him with loudspeakers to surrender, but he refused.    

He adds that after Shekau there are many other Shekaus who dont die and dont change, and who follow his ways and teachings, and will remain until the Day of Judgement.    

He calls ISWAP deviants for not considering regular Muslim civilians apostates for living in territories controlled by the Nigerian government, which JAS describes as the general of the polytheists. This is a major difference between JAS (Boko Haram) and ISWAP, as the latter only sees the military, their auxiliaries, and Christians who dont pay the jizyah as legitimate targets. By contrast, JAS views everyone living under the apostate governments as legitimate targets, which is the reason behind the recent bombings in Maiduguri by JAS.    

The speaker then addresses ISWAP again, urging them to repent before JAS destroys them, and says that if they do, they will be safe from them.  

He then greets JAS fighters in all their provinces, the Mandara Mountains, and Sadikus mobile faction in the northwest and north central, as they renew their pledge of allegiance to Abu Umaymah. Children are visible among the group."

text:
"Do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. 

Shaykh Khalid al-Fulayj (may Allah free him) said in his explanation of as Sunah of al Barbahari:

Islam cannot be firmly established except upon complete submission to the texts of the Book and the Sunnah. So do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. 

Knowledge is not based on the minds and views of men; rather, it is with Allah and with His Messenger. So do not follow anything based on your desires, lest you deviate from the religion and exit Islam, then you would have no proof.

The Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) has clarified the Sunnah for his Ummah and made it clear to his companions. They are the Jamaat, and they are the great majority, and the great majority is the truth and its people. So whoever opposes the companions of Muhammad (salallahu alayhi wa salam) in anything of the Deen has disbelieved.

From this issue, an important principle is established: the texts of the Book and the Sunnah are not to be opposed by opinions, desires, tastes, spiritual feelings, or intellects. Rather, the stance of a Muslim regarding revelation is submission, belief, listening, obedience, following, and compliance, without disputing.

Then some ignorant people may come and narrate a hadith in Sahih al-Bukhari from Abu Hurairah in which the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) said:

If a fly falls into the vessel of one of you, let him immerse it, for on one of its wings is disease and on the other is cure.

Then the pretentious, the arrogant, the pseudo-intellectual, and the ignorant say: This contradicts reason. This is something impossible, how can an insect have disease in one wing and cure in the other?

We say: We believe in Allah, we believe in His Messenger (salallahu alayhi wa salam), we affirm, we hear, and we obey. Our Messenger does not speak from his own desire, rather, it is nothing but revelation revealed. So just as the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) informed us, we say: we hear and we obey.

As for you, by opposing the report of the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam):

If you know the report is authentic, then you have denied the Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) and committed disbelief by rejecting it.

And if you are ignorant, we teach you and establish the proof against you. If you persist upon your falsehood and misguidance, then you disbelieve in Allah the Most Great, because you have described the Messenger as a liar and claimed that his statement is not true.

But the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) does not speak from his own desire, rather, he speaks only revelation that is revealed.

So this is what is obligatory upon the Muslim: belief, listening, and obedience."

text:
(84) 
The Claim That Shaykh Al-Albani Was Overly Lenient and Shaykh Muqbil Overly Strict in Hadith Grading

Question: 
It is said about Shaykh Nasir Ad-Din Al-Albani that he was lenient in his authentication and weakening of hadiths, and about Shaykh Muqbil that he was overly severe. How true are these statements?

Sheikh Yahya Al-Hajoori
As for the claim that Shaykh Al-Albani was lenient in authentication and weakeningthis is incorrect. How could that be? He never weakened a hadith without providing clear evidence for its weakness. He was extremely meticulous in weakening hadiths. His book Silsilah Adh-Daifah (The Chain of Weak Hadiths) stands as a testament to this, showing his great precision. Regarding authentication, he would sometimes exercise his own reasoning (ijtihad) on certain hadiths, citing various chains and applying his own methodological principles. He might say, "This hadith, through these chains, is hasan in my view." This is similar to what Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar does in Bulugh Al-Maram, where he says, "This hadith is hasan," even though some other hadith masters or researchers may disagree. The same can be said for Az-Zaylai and other scholarsthis is a normal matter of scholarly difference.

It is true that some hadiths in Silsilah As-Sahihah (The Chain of Authentic Hadiths) which he deemed authentic are not accepted by some other scholars. However, in reality, his ijtihad was very strong, and he provided the Muslim Ummah with an immense treasure that neither a scholar nor a student can do without. He remains a mujtahid, like other mujtahids in the field of hadith. A student of knowledge should still examine the hadiths, even those found in Silsilah As-Sahihah or Irwa Al-Ghalil, and form their own conclusion based on what they see, even if it aligns with Shaykh Al-Albanis ruling. If a student feels they have not reached the level to judge a hadith as hasan or sahih as the Shaykh did, they can simply hold their own informed opinion. And all praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

As for the claim that Shaykh Muqbil was severe in authentication and weakeningthere is no doubt that he was extremely meticulous. The "severity" here simply refers to his rigorous precision. Shaykh Muqbil would scrutinise many hadiths in light of hidden defects (ilal). I once asked him directly: "O Shaykh, the hadiths you included in As-Sahih Al-Musnad, did you examine them for hidden defects?" He replied, "I mostly examine them for hidden defects." He continued this practice throughout his life, constantly reviewing As-Sahih Al-Musnad against books of ilal. If Al-Hafidh Ad-Daraqutni, Abu Hatim, Abu Zurah, Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar, or any of the hadith masters stated that a hadith had a defect, Shaykh Muqbil would either remove it from his collection or keep it with a note indicating its defect.

In my observation, the criticisms regarding authentication directed at our Shaykh (Muqbil), may Allah have mercy on him, are far fewer than those directed at Shaykh Al-Albani. This is worth noting, considering the vast number of hadiths that Shaykh Al-Albani ruled upon compared to the number that our Shaykh ruled upon.

text:
4. Shaking hands with non-Mahram (unrelated) women: This is an affliction that has become widespread, and no one has escaped it except those whom Allah has shown mercy. It is prohibited due to what was narrated by Abdullah bin Al-Shikhkhir who said: I heard Ma'qil bin Yasar say: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (For one of you to be stabbed in the head with an iron needle is better for him than to touch a woman who is not permissible for him.)  Narrated by Al-Tabarani in Al-Mu'jam Al-Kabeer (486).
Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And in the hadith, there is a severe threat for the one who touches a woman who is not permissible for him; thus, it contains evidence for the prohibition of shaking hands with women, because that is undoubtedly included in touching.)  As-Silsilah As-Saheehah (1/448).
> 
> (So this hadith indicates explicitly the prohibition of touching a non-Mahram woman, and from this is: shaking hands; as the Lawgiver has attached a severe threat to touching a woman who is not permissible for him, which is: being stabbed with an iron needle. Attaching a severe threat to an action indicates its prohibition.)  Al-Musarahah fi Ahkam Al-Musafahah (p.62).
> 
5. Free mixing of men and women: This often occurs in parks and recreational areas, and there is much evidence regarding the prohibition of the free mixing of men and women, so the Muslim must be wary of that. From Abu Usaid Malik bin Rabi'ah, may Allah be pleased with him, that he heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saywhile he was coming out of the mosque, and men and women had mixed in the street: (Draw back, for you must not walk in the middle of the street; keep to the sides of the street. So women used to cling to the wall so much that their garments would catch on the wall due to their clinging to it.)  Narrated by Abu Dawud (5272).
Sheikh Muhammad bin Ibrahim, the former Grand Mufti of the Saudi Lands, said:
> (And the point of evidence is: that if the Messenger of Allahpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyprevented mixing in the street because it leads to temptation, then how can it be said that mixing is permissible elsewhere?!)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (10/42).
> 
Ali bin Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, said: (Do you not feel ashamed or have jealousy? For it has reached me that your women go out into the markets, crowding the 'Ulooj (1).)  Narrated by Ahmad (1118).
(1) Al-'Ulooj: Plural of 'Ilj, which refers to a disbelieving man of non-Arab descent.
Sheikh Al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And it was from the Sunnah of the Prophetpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyand the Sunnah of his successors to separate men and women... because the mixing of one group with the other is a cause for temptation; for when men mix with women, it is like mixing fire and wood.)  Al-Istiqamah (1/360).
> 
6. Women leaving their homes perfumed and scented: This action is not permissible; rather, it has come in the hadith that whoever does this is a fornicator. From Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari who said: The Messenger of Allah - peace and blessings be upon him - said: (Any woman who puts on perfume and passes by a people so they can smell her fragrance, is a fornicator.)  Narrated by An-Nasa'i (9361), and Ibn Khuzaymah (1681), and Al-Albani said: Its chain of narration is Hasan (good).
Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (Al-Haytami mentioned in 'Az-Zawajir' 2/37 that a woman leaving her home perfumed and adorned is among the major sins, even if her husband permitted her.)  Jilbab Al-Mar'ah Al-Muslimah (p.139).
>

Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said: > (And in the hadith, there is a severe threat for the one who touches a woman who is not permissible for him; thus, it contains evidence for the prohibition of shaking hands with women, because that is undoubtedly included in touching.) As-Silsilah As-Saheehah (1/448). > > (So this hadith indicates explicitly the prohibition of touching a non-Mahram woman, and from this is: shaking hands; as the Lawgiver has attached a severe threat to touching a woman who is not permissible for him, which is: being stabbed with an iron needle. Attaching a severe threat to an action indicates its prohibition.) Al-Musarahah fi Ahkam Al-Musafahah (p.62). > 5. Free mixing of men and women: This often occurs in parks and recreational areas, and there is much evidence regarding the prohibition of the free mixing of men and women, so the Muslim must be wary of that. From Abu Usaid Malik bin Rabi'ah, may Allah be pleased with him, that he heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saywhile he was coming out of the mosque, and men and women had mixed in the street: (Draw back, for you must not walk in the middle of the street; keep to the sides of the street. So women used to cling to the wall so much that their garments would catch on the wall due to their clinging to it.) Narrated by Abu Dawud (5272). Sheikh Muhammad bin Ibrahim, the former Grand Mufti of the Saudi Lands, said: > (And the point of evidence is: that if the Messenger of Allahpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyprevented mixing in the street because it leads to temptation, then how can it be said that mixing is permissible elsewhere?!) Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (10/42). > Ali bin Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, said: (Do you not feel ashamed or have jealousy? For it has reached me that your women go out into the markets, crowding the 'Ulooj (1).) Narrated by Ahmad (1118). (1) Al-'Ulooj: Plural of 'Ilj, which refers to a disbelieving man of non-Arab descent. Sheikh Al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allah have mercy on him, said: > (And it was from the Sunnah of the Prophetpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyand the Sunnah of his successors to separate men and women... because the mixing of one group with the other is a cause for temptation; for when men mix with women, it is like mixing fire and wood.) Al-Istiqamah (1/360). > 6. Women leaving their homes perfumed and scented: This action is not permissible; rather, it has come in the hadith that whoever does this is a fornicator. From Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari who said: The Messenger of Allah - peace and blessings be upon him - said: (Any woman who puts on perfume and passes by a people so they can smell her fragrance, is a fornicator.) Narrated by An-Nasa'i (9361), and Ibn Khuzaymah (1681), and Al-Albani said: Its chain of narration is Hasan (good). Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said: > (Al-Haytami mentioned in 'Az-Zawajir' 2/37 that a woman leaving her home perfumed and adorned is among the major sins, even if her husband permitted her.) Jilbab Al-Mar'ah Al-Muslimah (p.139). >

text:
"Surely, anyone who knows of the actions of Muhammad al-Eesa, and ascribes Islam to him  let alone believes him to be a senior scholar [of Islam]  has not smelt the scent of Islam. This goes without mentioning those that fund and promote his activities of heresy. (i.e., MBS and co.), and how that itself is enough proof for the Riddah of these enemies of Islam.

Two possible options for Fawzan:

1) He knows Muhammad al-Eesa is a Murtad, but cant say anything because hes afraid of those who inserted him in this position (i.e., MBS & co.). If he cant call out or fix such blatant Kufr, then his appointment is nothing more than a spectacle.

2) He doesnt know the reality of Muhammad al-Eesa, and thus affirms Islam for him. The problem with this, is that how can a Mufti of a land, be so ignorant of who and whats going on in that very land. Moreover, how can the leader of the committee of senior scholars, be so ignorant of the blatant and out in the open Zandaqa of the very members of that committee. Again, it just goes back to his appointment being nothing more than a spectacle.

A shame, Fawzan who is on the doorstep of his coffin, may Allah destroy him  and only Allah Knows when he will perish  cant even take a simple stand of truth, publicly, so that the truth is not clouded nor lost. What will he meet Allah with, to counter his decades worth of legitimizing the thrones of the Tawagheet who fight Islam and the Muslims?!

His current trajectory, and we ask Allah for Salmah, appears to follow in the footsteps of a [Temu] Balm, and be like how Allah Describes:

                             

And had We willed, We would surely have elevated him therewith but he clung to the earth and followed his own vain desire. So his parable is the parable of a dog: if you drive him away, he lolls his tongue out, or if you leave him alone, he (still) lolls his tongue out. Such is the parable of the people who reject Our Ayt (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). So relate the stories, perhaps they may reflect. [7:176]

What a terrible fate awaits the evil scholars that legitimize the Tawagheet. Verily, they themselves are Tawagheet,   ."

(56)
Fond Memories 

In Dammaj, there were two main masjids: the central masjid, where all the students gathered for Sheikh Yahyas daily lessons, and another masjid located in the mazraah (farmlands). 

During Ramadhan, these two masjids would engage in a friendly competition to see who could complete the most khatmahs.

One night, during class, a note was presented to Sheikh Yahya informing him that the brothers in the mazraah had completed Surah Al-Baqarah, Aal Imran, and An-Nisaa all in a single night. After praising them and invoking blessings upon their efforts, the sheikh went on to clarify that the Prophet     recited all three of these Surahs in just one rakah.

"Central Bank Official:
Interest System Stopped in All Commercial Banks

Dr. Lutfullah Khairkhwa, Director of Banking and Financial Services at Da Afghanistan Bank, announced that the practice of usury has been eliminated across all commercial banks operating in the country.

According to Khairkhwa, the national banking system is currently undergoing a complete transition away from interest-based transactions toward a fully Islamic banking model.

It may be recalled that Da Afghanistan Bank has previously affirmed that the payment or receipt of interest (riba) is forbidden under Islamic banking principles, and has been pursuing a phased conversion of the countrys conventional banking system to Sharia-compliant banking."

text:
"THIS IS NOT THE END

Shaykh Suliman al Alwaan (may Allah free him) said:

The fall of Dr al-Salm, Baghdad, and other Islamic lands is not the end of the world nor the end of Islam. Let the gloaters be disgraced. The future belongs to this deen, and soon the last part of this Ummah will be joined to its earliest part. So we must not weaken, nor should our efforts cease in supporting Muslims everywhere. Allah has praised those who face trials and hardship with steadfastness and patience. He said: And when the believers saw the confederates, they said: This is what Allah and His Messenger promised us, and Allah and His Messenger spoke the truth. And it increased them only in faith and submission.

These trials and hardships that befall the Muslims are a test, a means to distinguish the wicked from the good, the righteous from the corrupt, and the truthful from the liar. The believers increase in faith, while the hypocrites and those who worship Allah on the edge (I.e. in doubt) weaken, fall into cowardice, and show gloating. They say, If they had obeyed us, they would not have been killed. Allah the Exalted said: And among the people is he who worships Allah on an edge. If good befalls him, he is content with it; but if a trial touches him, he turns back on his face. He loses both this world and the Hereafter, that is the clear loss.

And He said: And among the people is he who says, We believe in Allah, but when he is harmed in Allahs cause, he considers the trial of the people as the punishment of Allah. But if victory comes from your Lord, they will surely say, Indeed, we were with you. Is not Allah most knowing of what is within the breasts of all beings?

And upon those whom Allah has entrusted with this great responsibility, such as the imams of mosques and others, is to urge the worshippers toward piety, repentance to Allah, giving them glad tidings of victory and empowerment, and making them aware of what the spiteful West intends, and what their brothers among the hypocrites, secularists, and the like are planning: a comprehensive assault on Islam and the Muslims, as they have openly declared a crusade.

They must work by every possible means to halt their advance and repel their aggression against the sanctities, wealth, and honor of the Muslims, and to strive against them with wealth, lives, and speech. For the Prophet  said: "Strive against the Mushrikeen with your wealth, your lives, and your tongues."
Reported by Imam Ahmad in his Musnad (12246), Ab Dwd (2504), and al-Nas (3098) through the narration of Hammad ibn Salamah, from Humayd, from Anas. Its chain is sound.

text:
Fatawa will never suffice for replacing Ahkaam.

A Fatwa is not transferable or enforceable.

A Hukm applies to all until an exception is established.

Laypeople deal with Muftis and their Fatawa as sacrosanct because they don't have access to Fuqahaa' and Ahkaam.

Whenever you hear a person begin to speak about Islamic law on the basis of Fatawa, know they are a layperson who has no standing on the matter.

Whenever you hear Muftis begin to deal with Islamic law on the basis of Fatawa or the unique, independent Ijtihad of specific scholars, know that they are bereft of Ijtihad themselves.

Safety from the corruption and mistakes of scholars is found in sticking to the Mu'tamad of the four schools of Fiqh.

ONLY IF a Mu'tamad view for a matter is not found because of a lack of precedent, then scholars are turned to for guidance.

Engaging in Ijtihad/Ifta while well-established Ahkaam exist as legal precedent is irresponsible and usually involve a corrupt agenda.

text:
"Three Duas to recite in every night in Ramadan

1. Dua for Forgiveness (Especially for Laylatul Qadr)

      

Allahumma innaka afuwwun tuhibbul afwa fafu anni

O Allah, You are Most Forgiving, and You love forgiveness, so forgive me.

2. Dua for Mercy and Guidance

           

Rabbana la tuzigh quloobana bada idh hadaytana wahab lana min ladunka rahmah

Our Lord, do not let our hearts deviate after You have guided us, and grant us mercy from Yourself.

3. Dua for Acceptance of Worship

      

Rabbana taqabbal minna innaka Antas-Sameeul-Aleem

Our Lord, accept from us. Indeed, You are the All-Hearing, All-Knowing."

"In a significant development, Saa'd, the former JNIM leader who defected recently to the Islamic State Sahel Province has released several audio statements explaining why he defected:

1) He accuses JNIM leaders of not applying Shariah (Islamic law) in its totality, despite him urging them to in several meetings with other senior JNIM and AQIM leaders, who found excuses in "politics and lack of ability" despite them "not hesitating to collect zakat from Muslims".

2) He says the JNIM accusation of IS-Sahel being "kharijites" is unfounded and has "no basis in the Qur'an or Sunnah".

3) He said he did not leave to IS looking for fame or money, because he was already "well-off" in the ranks of JNIM, and he only defected because he saw that the Islamic State's "actions matched with their words".

4 He accuses JNIM of negotiating peace deals with Benin and Ivory Coast, which he deems as not "befitting to a Muslim to seek reconciliation with apostates"."

"We sends our condolences and prayers to the family of Ashfaq Ahmad. A truly one of a kind individual, who worked tirelessly for justice, particularly for his son, Babar. 

This seemingly unassuming man who sought no position of authority or recognition, but simply justice for his son, put himself out there and shook the entire country and the Muslim community.

May Allah elevate his rank for all the work he did in his life, and reunite him with his recently deceased wife in the highest stations of Jannah. May he grant his family ease and strength during this period of loss. 

We encourage members of the community to keep them in your duaas, and attend the janazah when announced. 

The family will be receiving male visitors for condolences from 7pm to 9.30pm today, Wednesday 15 April, Thursday 16 April and Friday 17 April downstairs in Gatton Road Mosque, Gatton Road, London SW17 0EX."

No s si ustedes recuerdan el ralato bblico del Rey Salomn, contaba que dos madres se peleaban por un beb, ya que una accidentalmente haba ahogado al suyo y remplazado al mismo por el de la otra madre. 

El rey al ver esto, decidi que al nio lo partan en dos con una espada y le den la mitad a cada una. La madre falsa estuvo de acuerdo, la madre verdadera rog llorando que a su hijo se lo entreguen a la impostora para que siga con vida.

Que quiero decir con esto? Que a esta reverenda hdp no la vi llorar por su hijo ni por un segundo, solo se victimiz y falsific informes junto a los supuestos profesionales quitndole la tenencia al padre y cavando la tumba de su hijo.
