text:
""Iranians are exceptionally nationalistic about map boundaries compared to most populations, especially in the Middle East and other post-colonial regions.

We view our borders as ancient, not colonial, and take pride in the fact that they were not externally engineered in the twentieth century.

It is our civilizational inheritance.

It is not an overstatement to say that if Irans territorial integrity were ever seriously threatened, you would see monarchists, reformists, hardliners, etc., standing arm in arm to defend the country.

I have monarchist friends whom I know for a fact would get on a plane and risk imprisonment to offer themselves up to defend Iran.

For them, doing anything else would be unfathomable.

I feel the same way."
"
--------------

He speaks in a fictional we that exists only in Persian nationalist imagination, not in Irans lived reality. As a Sunni Iranian, this is immediately obvious, and it is obvious to anyone inside Iran who is not mainstream Persian. This includes even Shia groups who have largely lost a distinct identity and have been absorbed into Persian nationalism, such as many Azeris, Lors, and Caspian Iranians. 

This constant talk about Iran being uniquely special with natural borders is not some shared civilisational instinct. It is a standard modern Iranian nationalist myth, repeated so relentlessly that the average Iranian mistakes it for ancient truth, they actually believe they are special not realising they are not even the oldest civilisation in the Middle East. 

The irony is that both camps, i.e. Khomeinists and Shahists  that pretend to oppose each other fully embrace it Persian nationalist fairy tales. Yes, zealot hardcore  Khomeinists, the ideological heirs of the Safavids, and monarchists are united at the core. They quarrel over aesthetics, turbans versus crowns, but the substance is identical. Questioning the current borders is treated like apostasy, as if Iran itself were sacred. For many of them, it genuinely is, worshipped alongside God if not above Him (even in their everyday language one can observe that).

Everyone can destroy them with basic historical facts: the claim about ancient natural borders is laughable. It is pure myth making and cope on steroids. Geography has never magically produced legitimate states. Mountains and deserts were never sacred civilisational walls. They were zones of movement, trade, war, and overlap. The Zagros was a corridor for Arabs, Kurds, Lurs, Elamites (who were not Iranian in any ethnic sense), and Mesopotamian peoples for thousands of years. It was never a holy Iranian fence. Pretending otherwise is modern nationalist projection read backwards into history. But try to clarify these basics to them and they chimp out. 

Tey to explain to them that
Irans current borders did not descend from antiquity. They were violently hammered into shape under the  Safavids (Khomeini and Khamenei's salaf i.e. predecessors) and later reinforced by Qajar and Pahlavi centralisation through war, forced assimilation, and sectarian coercion as I already mentioned. Even when the rulers were Turco Persian, like the Safavids and Qajars, the project remained Persian nationalist and Shia supremacist, using sect and Persian identity to crush minorities, especially Sunnis. Borders shifted constantly and Sunnis and non Persian peoples were absorbed by force, not consent. For Sunnis, Kurds, Baloch, Arabs, and others, these borders were never inherited with pride. Never. They were imposed with blood, exclusion, and humiliation. This is precisely why both Khomeinists and Pahlavists are rightfully deeply resented among these communities.

What is telling is that this Iranian nationalist arrogance comes from insecurity. A multi ethnic state that does not match its ethnic or linguistic reality needs a myth of inevitability to survive. So conquest is rebranded as geography and coercion as civilisation. Older Middle Eastern peoples do not need this fantasy to justify themselves. Iranian nationalism does, because without it the entire story collapses.

The idea that monarchists, reformists, and hardliners would all unite to defend this map only proves the point. Different costumes, same ideology.

What makes this worse is not ignorant masses repeating these myths, but an Iranian elite that actively manufactures and teaches them to children as unquestionable fact. I heard these fairy tales constantly growing up, alongside open contempt for Arabs and Afghans and absurd fantasies about Iran belonging next to Austria and Switzerland. This is not pride. It is denial dressed up as history.,49.0

text:
A Concise Summary on the Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid 

Sheikh Saleem Al-Hilli afidhahullh states:

All praise is due to Allh, Lord of the Worlds, and peace and blessings be upon the Master of the Messengers, Muammad, and upon his family, companions, and those who follow them with excellence until the Day of Judgment.

My dear brothers and sisters...There has been much discussion regarding this matter (i.e. Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid). Some have asked: Does one prayer suffice for the other, or is it obligatory for a Muslim to perform both the Eid and Jumuah prayers?

Many who have spoken on this issue have done so without the requisite qualification to understand the evidences transmitted from the Messenger of Allh . This is because they approach the matter based on a flawed methodology: they first adopt a belief, then seek out supporting evidence. This is a mistake! The correct methodology is to derive beliefs from evidence, not to impose ones beliefs or schools of thought upon the evidence.

Therefore, I shall briefly present the adths and reports attributed to the Messenger of Allh  and the noble Companionsmay Allh be pleased with themthen explain the positions of the recognised schools of Fiqh on this issue, relying upon Allh Almighty.

So I say, with Allhs guidance, that there are both prophetic traditions (marf) and reports from the companion (mawqf) on this matter.

The first is the adth of Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allh be pleased with him), in which Muwiyah ibn Ab Sufyn (may Allh be pleased with him) asked him: Did you witness the Messenger of Allh  having two Eids occur on the same day? He replied, Yes. He asked, What did he do? Zayd said, He performed the Eid prayer, then granted a concession regarding the Jumuah prayer, saying: Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray. This adth was narrated by Amad, Ab Dwd, An-Nas, Ibn Mjah, Ad-Drim, and Al-Hkim, and it is authentic.

The second adth is from Ab Hurayrah (may Allh be pleased with him), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we will indeed perform the Jumuah. This adth was also narrated by Ab Dwd and Ibn Mjah, and it is authentic.

The third adth is from Ibn Umar (may Allh be pleased with him), who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh . He led the people in prayer, then said: Whoever wishes to attend the Jumuah may do so, and whoever wishes to refrain may do so. This adth was narrated by Ibn Mjah. A-abarn also transmitted it with the wording: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh  on the day of Eid Al-Fir and Friday. The Messenger of Allh  led them in the Eid prayer, then turned to face them and said: O people, you have achieved good and reward. Indeed, we are holding the Jumuah. Whoever wishes to join us may do so, and whoever wishes to return to his family may do so. This narration is also authentic.

The fourth adth is from Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them both), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we are gathering, in-sh-Allh. This adth is also authentic and narrated by Ibn Mjah.

From the reports of the Companions: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the day. Then we proceeded to attend Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we mentioned this to him, he said: He acted in accordance with the Sunnah. This was reported by Ab Dwd, and by Ibn Khuzaymah with a different wording and with an addition: Ibn Az-Zubayr said: I saw Umar ibn Al-Khab, when two Eids coincided, do the same.

My dear brothers and sisters...There has been much discussion regarding this matter (i.e. Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid). Some have asked: Does one prayer suffice for the other, or is it obligatory for a Muslim to perform both the Eid and Jumuah prayers? Many who have spoken on this issue have done so without the requisite qualification to understand the evidences transmitted from the Messenger of Allh . This is because they approach the matter based on a flawed methodology: they first adopt a belief, then seek out supporting evidence. This is a mistake! The correct methodology is to derive beliefs from evidence, not to impose ones beliefs or schools of thought upon the evidence. Therefore, I shall briefly present the adths and reports attributed to the Messenger of Allh and the noble Companionsmay Allh be pleased with themthen explain the positions of the recognised schools of Fiqh on this issue, relying upon Allh Almighty. So I say, with Allhs guidance, that there are both prophetic traditions (marf) and reports from the companion (mawqf) on this matter. The first is the adth of Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allh be pleased with him), in which Muwiyah ibn Ab Sufyn (may Allh be pleased with him) asked him: Did you witness the Messenger of Allh having two Eids occur on the same day? He replied, Yes. He asked, What did he do? Zayd said, He performed the Eid prayer, then granted a concession regarding the Jumuah prayer, saying: Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray. This adth was narrated by Amad, Ab Dwd, An-Nas, Ibn Mjah, Ad-Drim, and Al-Hkim, and it is authentic. The second adth is from Ab Hurayrah (may Allh be pleased with him), in which the Messenger of Allh said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we will indeed perform the Jumuah. This adth was also narrated by Ab Dwd and Ibn Mjah, and it is authentic. The third adth is from Ibn Umar (may Allh be pleased with him), who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh . He led the people in prayer, then said: Whoever wishes to attend the Jumuah may do so, and whoever wishes to refrain may do so. This adth was narrated by Ibn Mjah. A-abarn also transmitted it with the wording: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh on the day of Eid Al-Fir and Friday. The Messenger of Allh led them in the Eid prayer, then turned to face them and said: O people, you have achieved good and reward. Indeed, we are holding the Jumuah. Whoever wishes to join us may do so, and whoever wishes to return to his family may do so. This narration is also authentic. The fourth adth is from Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them both), in which the Messenger of Allh said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we are gathering, in-sh-Allh. This adth is also authentic and narrated by Ibn Mjah. From the reports of the Companions: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the day. Then we proceeded to attend Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we mentioned this to him, he said: He acted in accordance with the Sunnah. This was reported by Ab Dwd, and by Ibn Khuzaymah with a different wording and with an addition: Ibn Az-Zubayr said: I saw Umar ibn Al-Khab, when two Eids coincided, do the same.

text:
"**Shaykh Abd al-Rahman al-Barrak on UK Student Loans**

The Shaykh was asked about student loans in the UK with these features:

 Issued by the government to support students  
 Interest is applied from the moment the loan is taken  
 Repayment starts after graduation and employment, if income reaches a threshold  
 If income is low, repayment is not required  
 The loan may be written off after 30 years, or due to death or inability to work  

Verdict from the Shaykh:

It is not permissible to take this loan.

Reason:

 It involves interest paid by the borrower  
 It is a complicated contract with many conditions and issues  

Even with income-based repayment and possible write-off, the presence of interest means it should be avoided entirely."

text:
Ibn Rajab mentions in Ahwl al Qubr that some scholars mentioned that these 3 are singled out because:

The grave is the first stage of the Hereafter, offering a sample of the Day of Judgments consequences, not the full punishment/reward. 

Sins fall into two categories:
1.Rights of Allah (primary example: prayer)
2.Rights of servants (primary example: sanctity of life)

In the barzakh (the intermediate phase between death and Judgment), people are are not judged for the major sins themselves, but for their preludes and causes of lacking in them:

The prelude to prayer is to purify oneself of impurity and filth 

The prelude to violating the sanctity of life is slander and backbiting

So if a person failed to pray properly or violated the sanctity of a persons life, he will see the punishment for that on the Day of Judgment, but since these three (impurity, slander, backbiting) are lesser harms and foundational causes of these major sins, punishment in the grave begins with them; serving as an early consequence before the full reckoning on the Day of Judgment.

Either Yahya Silmi received this statement from idle, malicious opponents of the call of Imam Muqbil ibn Hadi Al-Wadii, which has filled the plains and mountains, or he heard something similar to what Abdullah Al-Bukhari statedthat Sheikh Muqbil repented from the methodology of khuruj in the sense of his words two months before his deathand due to his poor command of Arabic and ignorance, he assumed they were accusing Sheikh Muqbil of saying that Saudi Arabia is a kafir country. It is sufficient to show a persons misguidance that he be a liar in what he transmits or inarticulate in his speech, for the inarticulate cannot express what is in his heart nor clarify what he intends, thus falling into something worse than what he wishes to deny. His situation is like what some of the Salaf said about Umar ibn Ubayd: Your shortcoming came from your poor Arabic, when he said, It is incumbent upon Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, to fulfill the threat. So I advise Yahya Silmi to fear Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, and I advise him to be humble and to seek knowledge.

The second matter: His statement, He sat with its scholars and they convinced himwhat did they convince him of? The fact is that it was conveyed to him, may Allah have mercy on him, that there was some injustice in Saudi Arabia and such things, so he would condemn that specific injustice that was reported to him; otherwise, he remained upon the Salafi creed. Evidence for this is that Sheikh Ibn Baz, Sheikh Ibn Uthaymin, Sheikh Rabee, Sheikh An-Najmi, and Sheikh Al-Albaninone of them, not in a single letter, accused Sheikh Muqbil of holding the opinion of the Khawarij or the takfiris, despite their knowledge of what he said or uttered, whether in what he wrote in Gharat Al-Ashirat or what he wrote in Al-Makhraj min Al-Fitnah or what he stated in other books. Then he retracted, may Allah have mercy on him, before his death; he retracted from all statements he had made about Saudi Arabia in that tape entitled My Observations in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia when he saw and heard the good in the country, as he explained the reason for his retraction.

The point of this introduction is: I declare this transmission and this statement from Yahya Silmi to be false. Sheikh Muqbil did not declare the Saudi government to be disbelievers nor did he say it is a kafir country. Scholars condemned Sayyid Qutb when he called it the Jahiliyyah of the twentieth century, and then Yahya Silmi comes and attributes such a statement to an imam of the Salafi call and a reviver of the Prophetic Sunnah, claiming that he used to say Saudi Arabia is a disbelieving country. So check your sources, O you who fabricates! 
{ } 
Cursed be the liars! 
Review the Arabic language and master it. If something is unclear to you, be humble and ask until you understand. But to become a fabricator who falsely attributes statementsand to whom?! To an imam known for the soundness of his creed and methodology! We did not accept from Abdullah Al-Bukhari nor from anyone else who claims that Sheikh Muqbil repented from khuruj. We say that Sheikh Muqbil never held the view of khuruj, nor do we accept that, neither at the beginning of his pursuit of knowledge nor at the end. Sheikh Muqbil was a Sunni Salafi from the time Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, enlightened him with the Book and the Sunnah. He denied this about himself, truthfully in what he said and conveyed, that he was never with Juhayman at any time, nor was he a supporter of him; rather, he condemned him, and so on. I refer to what I wrote in my book Awn Al-Bari fi Bayan Hizbiyyati Ibnay Mari wa Man Jara Majrahum wa Radd Takharusat Abdullah Al-Bukhari, and added to it is the refutation of the fabrications of Yahya Silmi Al-Saylani.

text:
In a Al-Bukhr, from Ab Ubayd, the freed slave of Ibn Azhar, who said: I witnessed the two Eids with Uthmn ibn Affn on a Friday. He performed the prayer before delivering the sermon, then said: O people, today two Eids have coincided for you. Whoever among the residents of the outskirts (Al-awl) wishes to wait for the Jumu'ah, let him do so; whoever wishes to return home, I give him permission.

Based on these authentic prophetic traditions and reliable reports from the pious predecessors, the majority of scholars have concluded the following:

First: Whoever attends the Eid prayer is granted a concession from attending the Jumu'ah prayer. He may instead pray the Dhuhr prayer at its time. However, if he chooses to attend the Jumuah prayer with the congregation, that is more virtuous and better.

Second: Whoever did not attend the Eid prayer is not included in the concession. Therefore, the obligation of Jumuah remains upon him, and he must go to the masjid for the Jumuah prayer. If the minimum number required to establish Jumu'ah is not found, he performs Dhuhr instead.

Third: The Imm of the masjid is obliged to hold the Jumuah prayer that day, so that those who wish to attend it or those who did not attend the Eid prayer may do so. This is the explicit statement of the Prophet : Indeed, we are going to establish Jumuah prayer.

Fourth: Whoever attended the Eid prayer and avails himself of the concession not to attend Jumuah must pray Dhuhr. The Dhuhr prayer does not fall away under any circumstance, because what is lifted is the obligation of Jumuahnot of Dhuhr. Those upon whom Jumuah is not obligatorysuch as the sick, the traveller, women, and childrenare unanimously required by the scholars to perform Dhuhr.

Fifth: The opinion that whoever attends the Eid prayer is exempt from both the Jumuah and the Dhuhr prayers for that day is a weak opinion, though attributed to some Companions such as Ibn Az-Zubayr and Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them). This conclusion may be explained in several ways:

First, perhaps those who held this view were unaware of the adths that explicitly grant a concession from attending Jumuah, but affirm the obligation of Dhuhr for those who do not attend Jumuah.

Second, Ibn Az-Zubayr may have combined the Eid and Jumuah prayers at the time of the early Jumuah (before the sun reached its zenith). This is supported by a narration from Jbir ibn Abdullh (may Allh be pleased with him): We used to perform the prayer with the Messenger of Allh , then return and let our camels rest. This was reported by Muslim. Also, Sahl (may Allh be pleased with him) said: We would neither nap nor eat lunch until after Jumuah. This is the position of Imm Amad and Isq ibn Rhawayh (may Allh have mercy on them).

Additional evidence indicating that Ibn Az-Zubayr did this includes narrations such as: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the morning. We then went for Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we told him, he said: He followed the Sunnah. In another narration: A Friday and Eid coincided under Ibn Az-Zubayr, who said: Two Eids have come together in one day. So he combined them into one, prayed two rakahs early in the morning, and did not add anything until he prayed Ar.

This is a clear and explicit text that he included the Jumuah within the Eid prayer. And whoever performs the Jumuah does not then perform Dhuhr. Therefore, when this was reported to Ibn Abbs, he said: He acted upon the Sunnah.

This, dear brothers and sisters, is a concise yet beneficial explanation regarding the concurrence of Jumuah and Eid. I ask Allh, the Exalted, to have guided me to speak in accordance with the Sunnah and to have brought clarity on this issue to the general Muslim public...

text:
""Bir ifti, satmak iin bir eek ve bir keiyi ehre gtryordu. Keinin boynunda bir ngrak vard.

 hrsz, iftinin getiini grd. 

Birincisi dedi ki: Keiyi alacam, hem de ifti fark etmeden.

kincisi dedi ki: Ben de eeini alrm.

ncs ise dedi ki:  Ben de onu tamamen soyacam.

Birinci hrsz gizlice keiye yaklat, ngra karp eein kuyruuna balad ve keiyi alp bir tarlaya gtrd.

Yolun ortasnda ifti arkasna bakt ve keinin kaybolduunu fark etti. Onu aramaya koyuldu.

kinci hrsz yanna gelip ne aradn sordu. ifti keisinin alndn syledi. 

Hrsz dedi ki: Az nce burada, u ormanda bir adamn keiyle kotuunu grdm. Yetimen kolay olur.

ifti, eeini ona emanet edip keisinin peine dt. Hrsz ise eei alp gitti.

ifti ormandan geri dndnde eeini de bulamad. Alamaya balad ve yoluna devam etti. Yolda, bir batakln kenarnda alayan bir adam grd. Ona ne olduunu sordu. 

Adam dedi ki: ehre altn dolu bir uval gtryordum. Dinlenmek iin burada oturdum. Uyuyunca uvala arptm, suya dt.

ifti, Neden karp almyorsun? dedi.

Adam: Sudan korkuyorum, yzme bilmiyorum. Ama karana yirmi altn veririm. dedi.

ifti sevindi: Allah bunu bana, keim ve eeimin kaybn telafi etmek iin verdi. dedi.

Elbiselerini karp suya indi. Ama altn dolu uval bulamad. Sudan ktnda ise elbiselerini de bulamad.

Bu da nc hrszn iiydi. Oda, iftinin elbiselerini almay baarmt."

Bu hikye, insanlarn en ok zarar grd  durumu anlatr:

1-  Gaflet (Habersiz) annda arkadan gelen haner (darbe).

2-  Nasihat eder gibi grnp zehirleyen kiidir.

3- Duygulara oynayp insan kandran (aldatan) kiidir."

Uyuyunca uvala arptm, suya dt. ifti, Neden karp almyorsun? dedi. Adam: Sudan korkuyorum, yzme bilmiyorum. Ama karana yirmi altn veririm. dedi. ifti sevindi: Allah bunu bana, keim ve eeimin kaybn telafi etmek iin verdi. dedi. Elbiselerini karp suya indi. Ama altn dolu uval bulamad. Sudan ktnda ise elbiselerini de bulamad. Bu da nc hrszn iiydi. Oda, iftinin elbiselerini almay baarmt." text:
"A questioner asks: What is the ruling on offering congratulations and gifts on the occasion of Valentines Day, which falls in mid-February each year?

All praise is due to Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah and upon his family and companions. As for what follows:

This holiday is a festival of the kuffar and Mushrikeen. Even if it is called Valentines Day, it is a festival claimed by the Greeks, an ancient festival for a deity they worshipped besides Allah. 

Whether this holiday was for a deity they worshipped or merely for what they call and claim to be love, then congratulating others on it, celebrating this day, and honoring it is forbidden and not permissible.

We, the people of Islam, have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha. It is not permissible to congratulate others for any Eid besides these two. So congratulating others on this day and honoring it is not allowed; it is forbidden. You are not permitted to congratulate anyone on it, nor are you permitted to accept congratulations from anyone on this day. Rather, it is obligatory upon you and every Muslim to reject anyone who congratulates you on this day and to clarify to people that congratulating others on this day is forbidden and not permissible, and that it is a form of imitating the Kuffar and Mushrikeen in their false festivals, which are based on falsehood, disbelief, immorality, and wickedness.

How can they claim it is a day of love and magnify love while they are among the most hostile people toward Islam and Muslims, waging war against the religion of Allah and against the people of Islam? A Muslim should not be deceived by their labels or slogans. 

These Kuffar and wicked people are among the furthest from what is called love. They are the ones killing us, killing our brothers in Syria, Iraq, and everywhere, yet they claim this day is Valentines Day.

Therefore, it is not permissible to congratulate others on this day, nor is it permissible for a Muslim to honor it, give gifts on it, or accept gifts on it. It is a forbidden day; honoring it, celebrating it, and offering congratulations for such false festivals is forbidden.

We have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha, and these are the ones in which it is permissible to rejoice.

Likewise, commercial shops that sell gifts such as flowers, teddy bears, and similar items on this day, some people even change their pages or displays to red or use symbols associated with celebrating this day, selling flowers or anything that assists in honoring this day is not permissible. It is forbidden and is considered helping in falsehood. 

Allah says: Cooperate in righteousness and piety, and do not cooperate in sin and transgression.

Whoever sells such flowers with the intention of honoring that day, his sale is forbidden, and the money he earns from it is unlawful. Every body nourished by unlawful earnings, the Fire is more deserving of it. So it is not permissible to take such money, nor to sell flowers, change displays, alter slogans, or use symbols such as roses or the color red for this day, for these are forbidden and reprehensible matters.

It is obligatory upon a Muslim to honor the symbols of Allah, to honor the religion of Allah, to show disavowal of these Kuffar and wicked people, and to distinguish himself from them in their festivals and their false, misguided beliefs. For resemblance in outward matters leads to resemblance in inward matters.

We ask Allah for well-being and safety. 

Shaykh Khalid al Fulaij (may Allah free him)"

Likewise, commercial shops that sell gifts such as flowers, teddy bears, and similar items on this day, some people even change their pages or displays to red or use symbols associated with celebrating this day, selling flowers or anything that assists in honoring this day is not permissible. It is forbidden and is considered helping in falsehood. Allah says: Cooperate in righteousness and piety, and do not cooperate in sin and transgression. Whoever sells such flowers with the intention of honoring that day, his sale is forbidden, and the money he earns from it is unlawful. Every body nourished by unlawful earnings, the Fire is more deserving of it. So it is not permissible to take such money, nor to sell flowers, change displays, alter slogans, or use symbols such as roses or the color red for this day, for these are forbidden and reprehensible matters. It is obligatory upon a Muslim to honor the symbols of Allah, to honor the religion of Allah, to show disavowal of these Kuffar and wicked people, and to distinguish himself from them in their festivals and their false, misguided beliefs. For resemblance in outward matters leads to resemblance in inward matters. We ask Allah for well-being and safety. Shaykh Khalid al Fulaij (may Allah free him)"

text:
Ibn Qudmah   said : 

Some of the righteous predecessors would dig a grave for themselves. Whenever they became fatigued from performing righteous actions, they would lower themselves into the grave, stretch out, and say to themselves:

'O my soul, imagine that you are dead and now in the grave. What would you wish for?'

The soul would respond, 'I would wish to return to the duny (worldly life) so that I may perform righteous actions.'

The person would reply, 'You have been granted what you wished for. So get up and do righteous actions (before it is too late).

[Wasiyah 11]

"Throughout the years of benefitting from Shaykh Ahmad Musa Jibril (may Allah preserve him), one principle consistently shines through in his teachings:

His unwavering commitment to being a voice for the oppressed, prisoners, and those who cannot speak for themselves.

The Shaykh's profound concern for the Muslim ummah isn't also new; it's evident even in his writings from the early 2000s, revealing the depth of his love and compassion for the believers.

This is precisely why the recycled lies and slander against him are always futilethey cannot damage a da'wah built on such sincerity and care for the ummah.

As the Shaykh himself consistently reminds us: "The point is to ONLY please Allah alone, for you will never be able to please the creation."

May Allah preserve our Shaykh, increase him in beneficial knowledge, and accept his righteous efforts.

May Allah grant freedom and relief to all Muslim prisoners unjustly detained, strengthen their patience, and reunite them with their families.

May Allah humiliate and expose those who slander the people of haqq while defending the modern-day tawagheet who are enemies of Him and His religion. May Allah seal their tongues and protect the ummah from their misguidance."

text:
"Its genuinely heartbreaking that so many who claim Islam today don't even grasp the meaning of Shirk. 

We are talking about the greatest sin imaginable, the sin  will never forgive for whoever dies upon it without repentance, and the crime that condemns a person to eternity in the Fire. 

How have you not found the time to study a matter that decides your eternal fate?

What should be the clearest of all realities has become a subject of debate among people who belong to the very Deen that came to erase Shirk and establish Tawhid. 

If these people actually sat down, opened the Book of , and pondered its verses with the help of the great Mufassirun like Ibn Jarir, Ibn Kathir, al-Qurtubi, and al-Sadi, they would see that the entire Quran is Tawhid.

All of it is a condemnation of Shirk, a warning against its hideous nature, and a clear description of the end of those who practice it.

But instead of learning, they prefer to waste their lives attacking the Dawah of Sheikh Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab, may  have mercy on him. 

May  reward you on behalf of this Ummah, Sheikh, for you called to the pure Tawhid until the ignorant masses in their sheer lack of knowledge began to think you invented these core beliefs. 

They don't realize that your call was nothing more than the call of every Prophet and Messenger from the beginning of time.

I tell you this: Leave the books of the Sheikh aside for a moment, even though they are nothing but Ayat and Ahadith. 

Go directly to the Book of , the Revelation sent to this entire Ummah. Go to the scholars the Ummah has agreed upon for over a thousand years,Ibn Jarir and Ibn Kathir, and actually spend your time studying.

May  guide us and you to the Truth. 

May He protect us from deviation, whims, and the poison of doubts."

"Update on Sheikh Ali Al-Timimi:

Five years ago, Dr. Ali was released into home confinement, where he remains. At that time, he had served 15 years of a life plus 70-year sentence, all of which was in solitary confinement and under SAMS. 

In the summer of 2024, the lower court finally ruled in his favour and removed the life and 30 years from his sentence, leaving approximately 25 years of the original sentence. Since then, Dr. Ali has finally been allowed to appeal the original conviction and the remaining 25 years of that sentence. 

This is the first time in US legal history that a person has had to wait 20 years to have the opportunity to appeal his convictions.

Depending on how the appellate court rules, Dr. Ali will be cleared of the charges against him or compelled to return to prison to complete the remaining 25 years.

Given that he is in his 60s, a 25-year sentence is an effective death sentence. Please keep him in your prayers and inform as many well-wishers as possible.

 Idris Palmer (@AIdrisPalmer)"

text:
" Why One Raman Can Change Your Life!

 Sh Abu Idrees Muhammad Khn ( ).

 @AbuIdrees | @AstonMasjid 

 

  Hadith of two men who embraced Islam together; however, one would strive more than the other. He died during a military expedition. The other man lived a year more. alhah ibn Ubaydillh narrates that he saw in a dream that he was at the door of Paradise, and he saw these two men. A man from Jannah came and permitted the man who lived an extra year to enter first.
 The people were astonished that the one who died in a military expedition and strove in his Islam didnt enter Jannah first. The explanation given by the Prophet : The one who lived for an extra year reached another Raman, he fasted and prayed. The comparison between the two men in rewards is as vast as the heavens and the earth.
 The importance and virtues of Raman and the rewards that are to be gained during this month.
 The encouragement to capitalise in this month.
 The vast opportunities to gain Allhs Forgiveness in Raman.
 Let this month be for you and not against you.",

text:
" 

Asalamualaikum wa rahmatullahi wa barakaatuhu 

We hope and pray you are in the best of eemaan. The masjid has some great news to share, and we pray this assists in us pushing on and spreading the dawa more throughout Manchester. 

We are in the process of finalising a lease on a brand-new bookshop and dawa centre. Its not been easy to find something but we have tried to find the best place possible to accommodate both enough books, free literature as well as allowing small sittings and potentially lessons in the future by taken adjacent buildings.

We will announce the location in due course due to the sensitive nature of negotiations. However as we are at the stage of agreeing terms we thought to bring this to your attention so that you may contribute to such a blessed dawa centric activity. 

Sahl bin Sa'd (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet () said to 'Ali (May Allah be pleased with him), "By Allah, if a single person is guided by Allah through you, it will be better for you than a whole lot of red camels." Recorded in Al-Bukhari and Muslim (Riyad as-Salihin 1379).

In order to set up the bookshop we estimate that it will cost us approximately 42,000. This includes the legal fees, deposit on the building, the full refurbishment costs (electrical, plumbing, lighting, flooring, ceiling) furniture fixings, signage (external and internal) and then most important of all the books to stock and dawa material; as well as further stock to sell in order to raise funds to sustain the centre.

Please note our goal is only to make enough money so that it can carry out its main purpose, which is to call to Islam. Alongside the weekly city centre dawa stall we have, this will hopefully form a good central hub for people who are maybe new to Islam or want more information.
 
So we ask that you donate generously if you are able, to this noble cause of spreading Islam and teaching it to people - and rectifying the basics of the religion and assisting Muslims all over Manchester to return to the sunnah.

 :
MANCHESTER BOOKSTORE LTD
Account number: 21981312
Sort code: 04-06-05

 :


:
Manchesterbookstore@outlook.com

Bir yanmzda sistemin Krtleri yozlatrmak iin sokaa sald eteler, dier yanmzda ise kresel sistemin Krtleri slamdan ve kimliklerinden uzaklatrp; kitapsz, peygambersiz, Bat hayran, aile dman ve deerlerine yabanc hle getirmek isteyen malum rgt vard.

Allah (cc), beni ve benim gibi binlerce genci bu bataklktan; o sokaklarda kurunlanmay, ikence grmeyi ve bir mr zindanda kalmay gze alan farkl birok cemaatin eliyle kurtard. ayet slami cemaatlerin sokaktaki genlere aabeylik yapan, onlara kulluk vazifelerini hatrlatan genleri olmasayd; pek ok insan gayrimeru etelerin ve rgtlerin elinde heder olup giderdi. 

Genliin kurtuluu iin slami cemaatlerin sokaklarda daha grnr olmas lazmdr. Buyurun, sizler de ahitliklerinizi dile getirin."

""#ALERT: Al-Shabaab Leader Sheikh Ali Dheere Sends Son on Suicide Mission in Godka Jilicow Attack  5 October 2025

In a disturbing propaganda release timed for Eid, Al-Shabaab revealed that one of its senior figures sent his own son to participate in a suicide attack on the NISA detention facility at Godka Jilicow in Mogadishu on 5 October 2025.

The video identifies the young attacker as Abdirahman, the son of the groups spokesman Ali Mahmoud Rage, also known as Sheikh Ali Dheere. Analysts note that this is only the second time in Al-Shabaabs history that senior leadership has involved their own children in such missions. The first recorded instance was in 2013, when the son of former Al-Shabaab governor Yusuf Sheikh Isse, known as Kaba-kutukade, carried out a suicide bombing at the Turkish Embassy in Mogadishu."

text:
"How the Najdi scholars refuted the extremists

In the name of Allah, the Most Merciful, the Bestower of Mercy.

From: Abd al-Laf ibn Abd al-Ramn ibn asan
To: Abd al-Azz al-Khab

Peace be upon those who follow guidance, and upon the righteous servants of Allah.

To proceed:

I have read your letter and understood its contents, and what you intended by way of apology. However, you erred in your statement that what our shaykh, the father, denounced, namely your declaring the people of truth to be disbelievers, and your belief that you are correct, when you denied it.

You mentioned that your brothers from al-Naq are disputing with you and are arguing with you concerning us, and that they say that we have been silent regarding certain matters. And you know that they often mention this to attack our creed and to criticize our methodology. Even if they do not explicitly make Takfeer, they have approached its borders. So we seek refuge in Allah from misguidance after being upon guidance, from deviating away from the path of rectitude, and from blindness.

I witnessed, in the year 64, two men resembling you who were among the renegades in al-As. They abandoned the Friday prayer and the congregational prayer, and declared the Muslims of that land to be disbelievers. Their argument was similar to yours. They said: The people of al-As sit with Ibn Fayrz and mix with him and others like him, those who did not reject the ght by disbelieving in it, nor explicitly declare his grandfather to be a disbeliever who rejected the call of Shaykh Muammad, and did not accept it, and instead opposed it.

They both said: Whoever does not explicitly declare him to be a disbeliever is himself a disbeliever in Allah and has not rejected the ght by disbelieving in it. And whoever sits with him is like him. And upon these two false and misguided premises, they built rulings implying clear apostasy, such that they even abandoned returning greetings of Salam.

Their matter was raised to me, so I summoned them, threatened them, and spoke harshly to them. At first, they claimed that they were upon the creed of Shaykh Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb, and that his letters were with them. So I clarified their errors and refuted their misconceptions with what I had in that gathering.

I informed them that Shaykh Muammad is free from this belief and this path, and that he does not declare anyone to be a Kaafir except for those matters that the Muslims have unanimously agreed upon that makes one a Kaafir, such as major shirk, or rejecting the verses of Allah and His messengers, or any of that, after the proof has been shown to him and is properly conveyed. Such as making Takfeer on the one who worships the righteous, prays to them alongside Allah, and for making them His rivals in what He alone deserves in worship and authority over the creation.

This is agreed upon by the people of knowledge and faith. Every group among the followers of the various upheld Mazhabs has devoted a major chapter to this issue, mentioning its rulings, and that which causes apostasy and its implications, and explicitly mentioning Shirk. Ibn ajar even singled out this issue in a book he titled: al-Ilm bi-Qawi al-Islm (Informing about the Nullifiers of Islam).

These two Persians later showed repentance and remorse and claimed that the truth had become clear to them. Then they went to the coast and returned to that same doctrine. It reached us that they declared the leaders of the Muslims to be Kaafirs for communicating with the Egyptian rulers. Rather, they even declared as disbelievers those among the Muslim scholars who associated with those who communicated with them. We seek refuge in Allah from misguidance after being upon guidance and from relapse after rectitude.

We have also been informed about similar matters regarding you. And that you ....

Continued  "

text:
"Wisdom is to do what is correct, in the correct manner, at the correct time.

It has 3 pillars: 
1. Knowledge 2. Forbearance 3. Patience

The opposites: 
1. Ignorance 2. Recklessness 3. Haste

Thus, wisdom cannot be found in the ignorant, reckless, or hasty.

Imam Ibn al-Qayyim

text:
"A Note Regarding my Trip to the USA 8th - 13th April, 2026

It has once again been a truly enjoyable visit to the USA. I extend my sincere thanks to our beloved communities in Roanoke, Virginia, and Columbus, Ohio, for organizing and facilitating the events over the past few days. I am deeply grateful for their outstanding hospitality.

By the blessing of Allh, I had the opportunity to meet communities being cultivated upon the way of the Scholarshonouring them, loving them, and recognizing their rightful status. May Allh continue to grant them success.

The dawah in the USA is clearly growing. I ask Allh to bless the communities of Roanoke and Columbus, and all Salaf communities, as well as all our respected and noble brothersthe honourable dut and teachers in the USA who strive tirelessly to benefit the Muslims across the country. They teach the Quran and Sunnah, connect the people to the Scholars, and nurture within them love and respect for them: Ab Muhammad al-Maghrab, Abul Hasan Mlik, Hasan Somali, Abul-Abbs Ms Richardson, Anwar Wright, Umar Quinn, Rasheed Barbee, Raha Batts, Jaml Finch, Hamza AbdurRazzq, Mustaf George, Ab Ansa Abdul Hakeem, and others among the teachers upon the same commendable path whom I may have missed.

For decades, our dawah in the West has been firmly built upon returning to the Scholarsmaintaining strong ties with them, seeking their guidance in our affairs, and recognizing our position beneath them. By the permission of Allh, if we remain steadfast upon this path, we will continue to grow and progress, our dawah will only become stronger, and we will find the sweetness of brotherhood, love and unity between us.

As for those who are no longer able to uphold this way, and have instead opened doors to corruption and misplaced boldness against the scholars that we long worked within the dawah to keep firmly shut for yearsand in fact rebuked opposers who often attempt to open themthen do not concern yourselves with such individuals or their supporters issuing lengthy and meandering justifications for their weak and erroneous positions lacking resolveat a time when nothing but firm resolve itself is the requirement.

Ab Muadh Taqweem

25th Shawwl 1447h
13th April, 2026

  "

text:
"The adversaries of tawd, the promoters of moral decay, and those calling for liberation from values, ethics, and divine commands and prohibitions; such people are increasing in this age, not diminishing. They proclaim that whoever merely says l ilha ill Allh is a believer, even if he abandons the Sharah of Allah entirely!

In their view and creed, rulings pertain only to what lies in the heart, not to outward actions. And the more pretentious among them insist that l ilha ill Allh does not encompass all aspects of life. The result of this corrupt ideology has been the spread of wickedness across the earth, the suspension of jihd in the path of Allah, and the rise of shirk, biddah, and political, economic, intellectual, and social deviations among the Muslims, whereby the true meanings of the Sharah have been lost.

Thus the Murji doctrine fused with the secular mindset that seeks to sever religion from life and life from religion. Many people came to believe that worship is confined to ritual acts performed in the home and the mosque, and that religion has no place in governance or politics. They roll off their tongues the word of unbelief: Render unto Allah what is Allahs, and unto Caesar what is Caesars.

al-Allmah Sulaymn al-Alwn - Al inna nasra Allhi qarb"

text:
"The devil-hearted ruler... 

There are many wicked Khariji Muslims who deliberately twist the narrations regarding the obedience to rulers. Or, they cite the ones that they allege support their revolutionary tendencies and ignore the ones which expose them and their lies. 

Be careful of these people. 

If you live in Muslim country that has a Muslim ruler, then listen and obey in that which is good and don't obey him in disobedience.

text:
The young lion , mujhid and martyr of Chechnya 

Muhammed Yutaev (Chechen:  ), also known as Magomed Yutaev, was a young Chechen fighter born in 1980 in Shalazhi, a village in Chechnya. He is remembered for his role in the First and Second Chechen Wars during his adolescence.

In early 1995, at the age of 14, Yutaev voluntarily joined the popular militia of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria to defend against the Russian invasion that began in December 1994. His commitment to the cause led to his selection for participation in significant operations, including Shamil Basayev's raid on Budyonnovsk in June 1995, a pivotal event in the war.

On May 21, 1996, Yutaev gave an interview to a Chechen presidential broadcaster, which brought him national recognition. Later that year, in August, he was involved in the Battle of Grozny, also known as Operation Jihad, which contributed to the partial liberation of the capital and was instrumental in securing victory in the First Chechen War.

At the end of 1999, during the onset of the Second Chechen War, Yutaev was captured by Russian forces. It is believed that he died due to torture suffered in Russian prisons . He is considered a martyr by Chechen separatists and was posthumously awarded the title "Qoman Turpal" (Hero of the Nation).

Yutaev's legacy endures as a symbol of youthful bravery in the Chechen resistance. His 1996 interview remains available online, serving as a testament to his dedication and the broader struggle for Chechen independence.

text:
"The Difference Between Dr al-Islm and Dr al-Kufr:

Ab Ysuf, the companion of Ab anfah, said:

A land is considered Dr al-Islm when the rulings of Islam are manifest in it, even if most of its inhabitants are disbelievers. And a land is considered Dr al-Kufr when the rulings of disbelief are manifest in it, even if most of its inhabitants are Muslims.
[al-Mabs by al-Sarakhs 10/144]

Ibn Mufli al-anbal said:

Every land in which the rulings of the Muslims prevail is Dr al-Islm, and if the rulings of disbelief prevail in it, then it is Dr al-Kufr. There is no third category beyond these two.
[al-db al-Shariyyah 1/213]

Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah said:

The majority said: Dr al-Islm is that land in which the Muslims reside and upon which the rulings of Islam are implemented. Whatever the rulings of Islam are not implemented upon is not Dr al-Islm, even if it is adjacent to it.
[Akm Ahl al-Dhimmah 1/266]

Al-Q Ab Yal al-Farr said:

Every land in which the dominance is for the rulings of disbelief over the rulings of Islam is Dr al-Kufr.
[al-Mutamad f Ul al-Dn, p. 276]

Al-Ksn said:

When the rulings of disbelief become manifest in a land, it becomes Dr al-Kufr; a land becomes Dr al-Kufr by the manifestation of the rulings of disbelief.
[Badi al-ani 9/4375]

Muammad ibn al-asan al-Shaybn said:

Every land is attributed either to Islam or to disbelief. A land is attributed to Islam when its rulings are implemented in it, and it is attributed to disbelief when its rulings are implemented in it. Just as one says Paradise is the abode of peace and Hell is the abode of ruin, due to the presence of peace in Paradise and ruin in Hell. Likewise, the manifestation of Islam or disbelief is by the manifestation of their rulings.
[Badi al-ani 9/4375]

Manr al-Buht said:

Hijrah is obligatory upon one who is unable to manifest his religion in Dr al-arb, which is the land where the rulings of disbelief prevail.
[Kashshf al-Qin 3/43]

Al-Allmah amad ibn Atq said:

Whoever has familiarity with what the verifying scholars have established knows that when shirk becomes manifest in a land, prohibited matters are openly practiced, and the symbols of the religion are abolished, then it becomes a land of disbelief, its peoples wealth may be taken and their blood made lawful. The people of this land have gone further by openly insulting Allah and His religion, and they have established laws which they enforce upon the people, in opposition to the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of His Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam). And you know that this alone is sufficient to expel the one who does it from Islam.
[al-Durar al-Saniyyah 9/257]

The Mufti Muammad ibn Ibrhm l al-Shaykh said:

A land in which judgment is made by man-made law is not a land of Islam, and migration from it is obligatory. Likewise, if idoltary becomes manifest without being condemned or changed, then migration is obligatory. Disbelief, by its widespread prevalence and open manifestation, renders it a land of disbelief.
[Fatw Muammad ibn Ibrhm 6/188]"

text:
"Yasir Qadhi misses the mark againhe contradicts the Quran and opposes the Messenger of Allah () in his end justifies the means political philosophy. He screams out in exultation of the Rabbis and Jews, These are our brethren! These are our brethren! They are the REAL followers of Moses! They are the ones who make the Children of Israel proud!
This is not the belief of the Muslimsthis is not what the Qur'an teaches, nor is it from the teachings of the Prophet Muhammad (). The REAL followers of Moses (peace be upon him) believe in Jesus son of Mary as the servant and Messenger of God, and they believe in Muhammad () as the servant and final Messenger of Godthat is the religion of Islam. 
Allah () said in the Quran: And whoever desires other than Islam as religionnever will it be accepted from him, and in the Hereafter, he will be among the losers. (3:85) And He (the Most High) said: Verily, those who disbelieve (in the religion of Islam) among the People of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) and the Pagans will abide in the Fire of Hell. They are the worst of creation. (98:6) So, how are the Jews who disbelieved in Jesus and Muhammad (and disbelieved in the Books revealed to these Prophets) be the REAL followers of Moses?!
If they were [as Qadhi claims] the REAL followers of Moses (peace be upon him), they would follow the Prophet Muhammad () because that is what Moses (peace be upon him) would do, just as the Prophet () said: I have certainly come to you with that which is pure and clear proof... By the One in whose Hand is my soul, even if Moses (peace be upon him) was alive, he would have no choice but to follow me. (Imm Ahmad in al-Musnad, 3/387 no. 14859, graded authentic by al-Albn)
Furthermore, The Talmudic stories make fun of Jesus birth from a virgin, fervently contest his claim to be the Messiah... and maintain that he was rightfully executed as a blasphemer and idolater. (Jesus in the Talmud, Peter Schfer)
Our brethren believe in all of Gods Prophets including Moses, Jesus and Muhammad (peace be upon them all). The Prophet () said: By the One in whose hand is the soul of Muhammad, there is not a person from this nation, whether he is a Jew or a Christian, who hears of me, and then dies without believing in the message I was sent with except that he will be among the people of Hellfire. (Muslim, no. 153)"

text:
Genetic Profile Summary   (Male)

1. Overall Ancestry Profile (Modern Context)

This individual fits squarely within the Western North African (Maghrebi) genetic spectrum, with strong ties to populations from:

Morocco (especially urban/northern regions like Fs)

Tunisia

North African Jewish communities (Libyan, Tunisian, Algerian)

Core Ancestral Components:

North African (Maghrebi / Berber-derived): ~3034%

Levantine (Eastern Mediterranean): ~2030%

Southern European (Mediterranean): ~1317%

Egyptian-related: up to ~18% (model-dependent)

Arabian Peninsula: ~36%

Sub-Saharan African: trace amounts

 Interpretation:

This profile reflects a classic Western Mediterranean fusion zone, where indigenous North African ancestry is layered with:

Eastern Mediterranean gene flow (Levant)

Southern European input (especially island/coastal populations)

Minor trans-Saharan and Arabian influences

2. Deep Ancestry (Prehistoric Breakdown)

Upper Paleolithic (~25,000+ years ago)

Ancient West Eurasian: 96.13%

Ancient African: 3.87%

Closest affinity:

Caucasus-related populations (ancestral to later West Eurasians)

Mesolithic (~10,00015,000 years ago)

A three-way structure typical of the Southern Mediterranean basin:

Natufian (Levant): 36.64%

Anatolian Hunter-Gatherer: 35.34%

Caucasus Hunter-Gatherer: 20.28%

Minor components:

Ancient East African (Mota-like): ~6%

Trace Ancient North Eurasian

Interpretation:

This combination represents the core genetic foundation of Mediterranean and Near Eastern populations, later transmitted into North Africa through migration and diffusion.

3. Paternal Line (Y-DNA): R-Y16006

Lineage Overview:

Belongs to the R1a-Z93 branch, associated with Bronze Age expansions

Emerged approximately 1350 BCE

Migration Context:

Originates from a lineage that moved: 

From Eurasian steppe regions

Into Central Asia  Near East  North Africa

Notable Features:

Rare in North Africa but present through historical migrations

Found today in:

Eastern Europe

Near East

Egypt

Known associations:

Some Jewish Levite lineages

Occasional links to elite or structured male line expansions

 Interpretation:

This paternal line likely entered North Africa through:

Levantine or Jewish-mediated migration routes

Or broader Bronze/Iron Age population movements

4. Maternal Line (mtDNA): M1b2

Lineage Overview:

Deep-rooted lineage dating to ~10,000 BCE

Part of haplogroup M1, which is unusual because: 

It originates outside Africa

Then returns via back-migration into North Africa

Historical Associations:

Strongly linked to: 

Early North African populations

Iberomaurusian cultural horizon

Mediterranean coastal populations

Modern Presence:

North Africa

Southern Europe (Spain, Portugal, Italy)

 Interpretation:

This is a native North African maternal lineage with deep prehistoric continuity and Mediterranean connectivity.

5. Integrated Genetic Interpretation

 represents a textbook Western Mediterranean North African profile, defined by:

Indigenous Maghrebi (Berber-derived) ancestry

Strong Levantine genetic influence

Noticeable Southern European admixture

Minor but real African and Arabian inputs

Lineage Contrast:

Paternal line: intrusive, likely from Bronze Age or later migrations

Maternal line: deeply rooted in North Africa since the early Holocene

 Big Picture:

This individual reflects the historical layering of the Maghreb, shaped by:

Prehistoric Mediterranean populations

Neolithic and Bronze Age expansions

Phoenician, Roman, and later Islamic-era gene flow

Jewish diaspora integration

text:
Sayn Cumhurbakanm bir konuda sizi uyarmak zorundaym. mrm sizin davanza hizmet etmekle geti ama Gazze konusunda yanl yapyorsunuz. 

Gazzede insanlar diri diri yanyor, alktan can veriyor, bir avu gda ve su iin birbirlerini eziyorlar. 
Siz ise hl BM'den, AB'den ve dier zulm birliklerinden medet umuyorsunuz. u bir hakikat ki Mslman'n kardei yine baka bir Mslmandr. Tarih boyunca Mslmanlara yine sadece Mslmanlar yardm etmitir. Zira kfr tek millettir. Yani sizin arlarnz beyhudedir ve kafirlere bel balamak Mslmanlarn sadece izzetini zedeler. Siz ve bu yazy okuyanlar unu dnebilir ya da syleyebilir: "u anda gcmz az ve ekonomimiz zayf." Maalesef bu dnce slm deildir. Bu dnce Allah' hesaba katmayan bir dncedir. Rabbimiz ayetinde " Neden Allah yolunda ve zayf dm erkek, kadn ve ocuklar iin savamyorsunuz? yle zayflar ki; Ey Rabbimiz! Bizi, ahalisi zalim olan bu ehirden kar; bize kendi katndan bir sahip kl ve bize kendi katndan bir yardmc ver. derler. (Nisa 75. Ayet) buyuruyor. Bu ayet vb. ayetler varken nasl oluyor da birtakm dnyevi kayglarla harekete gemiyorsunuz. Bu vaheti sizin knamalarnz ya da diplomatik giriimleriniz durduramaz. nk muhatap olduumuz varlklar insandan da aa bir topluluk ve bu aalklar yaptklar vaheti ibadet gryorlar.
slm, zulm karsnda ertelemeyi deil, kyam emreder!
Hadisi erifte Allah'n Resul:
Kim bir ktlk grrse, eliyle dzeltsin. Gc yetmezse diliyle, ona da gc yetmezse kalbiyle kar koysun. Bu, imann en zayf derecesidir. (Mslim, mn 78) buyuruyor. 
Biz hep imann en zayfna m talip olacaz. Biz, Bedir'deki 300 Mslmandan daha m azz ya da silahszz ki hep glenmeyi ve zaman hesaplar olduk. Kffara kar yaptnz hazrlk ALLAHn yardmndan daha m gl. Bu mmet ALLAHn yardm ile ne zalimleri devirdi. Hangi silah ALLAHn yardmndan daha gl olabilir. 

Bugn sessiz kalan, yarn zalimin orta olur!
Glenmeyi bekleyenler, zalimin mrn uzatr!
Halk icraat bekliyor.
Bu zulme susulan hergne Hem tarih hesap soracak hem de Rabbimiz.

Artk hamas aklama ya da knama deil, adm atn!
Unutmayn ki mazlumun feryad, liderin snavdr!
Ya imdi, ya asla Sayn Erdoan!
Bu millet sizi One Minute ile hatrlyor. Ama imdi sadece sz deil, fiil duru grmek istiyor!

Adalet ertelemeye gelmez. Zulm seyredilemez. G gelince deil, zulm varken susulmamaldr. 
Bu uyarm da u hadis mucibince yapyorum: "Sizden nceki mmetler, ilerinden biri gnah ilediinde, dieri onu uyarmazd. Sonunda hepsi helak oldu. (Eb Dvd, Melhim 17)" topluca helak olmamak adna yapmanz gereken islam devletleri bakanlarn toplayp sraili ve destekilerine kar topluca sava karar almanz. Sizle birlikte savamayacak devlet bakanlarn aklayn. Savaa girmeyecek korkak devlet bakanlarnn gerek yzn kendi halklar grsn. Siz Gazzeye yryn sadece Mslmanlar deil dnyann her kesinden farkl dinlere mensup milyonlar hatta yz milyonlar sizle birlikte Gazzeye girsin. SRALN Ate aarsa sava emri verin. 
Ya hep beraber lrz ya hep beraber diriliriz. 
slm  mmeti ve milletimiz adna seslendim. Selmetle.

text:
"I dont like to comment on spubs minions too much, since it can be likened to abusing the mentally handicapped, but some instances its a must to highlight just how illiterate they are. This Zindq Madkhali is attempting to refute a brother for allegedly misrepresenting, only to misrepresent it himself and refute himself. This is standard Madkhali refutation protocol. Copy and paste, cringe flex on social media, and hope the audience is just as illiterate as him.

This idiot fails to read the two words before the highlighted word. Which say:     meaning, Even if he doesnt believe [that this system of governance] is better than the governance of the Shariah].

This brain dead kid saw the word  and thought the author is conditioning believing a certain system of governance is better than the Shariah, for it to be Major Kufr, when in fact the author does the opposite. Lol, Miskeen. The real kicker for this illiterate kid, and perhaps the pen is lifted from him  is when you read the statement as a whole, when the author (Ibn Baz) is describing nullifiers of Islam:

      (1)                (2)                                       .

And included in that also is everyone who believes that it is permissible to govern by other than the Sharah of Allah in transactions, or prescribed punishments, or other than them  Even if he doesnt believe that [this system of governance] is better than the governance of the Shariah  because by that he would have permitted what Allah has forbidden by consensus. And everyone who deems permissible what Allah has forbidden  from that which is known of the religion by necessity, such as fornication, wine, usury, and judging [between the people] by other than The Sharah of Allah  then he is a disbeliever by the consensus of the Muslims.

Perhaps the Madkhali will persist in his idiocy and illiteracy, because they notice the word  again here at the beginning. The Madkhali trope is to say that  in this passage [and others] can only mean  with respect to the Shariah of Allah. The response to this only requires 3rd grade reading comprehension.

When the author first uses it, its (1)  , and when he uses it later on (2) its referring to  , and the proof for that, is he distinguishes between two different shariahs by using them to contrast one another.

The Madkhali reading of the text, and I use reading very loosely here: 

Its Kufr only if someone believes its allowed within the Shariah, to govern by other than the Shariah even if they dont believe their [innovated] shariah is better than the Shariah of Allah.

How can you ascribe something to the Shariah, yet at the same time distinguish the same thing you attributed to the Shariah, to another shariah?! The statement doesnt even make sense if read this way.

The reality is, the author distinguishes between two shariahs, so by basic reading comprehension, the use of  cant be the same for both instances, because if so, the latter use would contradict its former use.  Rather what is obvious and clearly the intent, is that the latter use of  qualifies the use of the former .

In this statement alone, the author highlights the mere action of [permanently] governing by other shariahs as Kufr, even if only in some matters, and that such an action is Kufr by consensus. He also highlights how scholars have used , which will be highlighted in a future video InshaAllah.

Madkhalis are by far the most intellectually handicapped of the claimants of Islam, only rivaled by Jahmis (modern-day Asharis)."

Madkhalis are by far the most intellectually handicapped of the claimants of Islam, only rivaled by Jahmis (modern-day Asharis)."

text:
"Regarding Pakistan / Afghanistan relations 

From my friend @LongshotIbrahim -- a brother who has written on and studied the subject considerably:

I have mentioned this before but in 1980s/1990s various Afghan factions, incl both Taliban and some of their foes, supported Pakistan and Kashmir and went out of the way to do so. This was while an increasingly pro-India US was running interference for India.

While Afg mujahideen in general backed Pakistan and Kashmir, outstanding case was Hikmatyar (Afg prime minister in 1993-94, then 1995-96), and his field commander Khalid Faruqi. Hizbul Mujahideen were trained and at least partly modelled on them for a decade or so.

Another major case was Arsala-Rahmani Daulat (prime minister 1994-95), who later joined Taliban and was murdered in 2012. A third was justice minister Jalaluddin Haqqani, father of today's Taliban interior minister Sirajuddin.

In general Taliban remained pro-Pakistan thru their first emirate. During the 1999 Pak war w India over Kashmir, Taliban prime minister Mohammad Rabbani offered to send thousands of fighters (but was declined bcos there wasnt really a manpower shortage).

At the end of 1999, Pak militants related to Kashmir jihad incl. Masood Azhar were released from Indian custody in a hostage exchange mediated, much to Pakistani glee, by Taliban Kandahar corps commander Akhtar Usmani.

Many of Kashmir-focused mujahideen also supported Taliban in late 1990s incl Saifullah Akhtar and AbdulJabbar Farooq. They fought directly under defence minister Ubaidullah Akhund and his lieutenant, today's Afghan security minister Ibrahim Sadar.

In east Afg, Taliban and Hizb and also Salafi networks were linked to Kashmir-focused mujahideen incl Salafi "Lashkar Taiba". One commander in Kunar who latee fought US at the famed Korengal valley, AbdulRahim, was a Hizbi turned Taliban but of Salafi persuasion, close to Taiba.

In 2001-02 after US invasion, Pakistan banned some of these groups (but tacitly tolerated others like Taiba). AbdulJabbar and Saifullah as well as Ilyas Kashmiri revolted against Pakistan, while Khalil and Azhar nervously walked a fine line.

In 2004, partly bcos of these rebels but also largely to distract US after Iran/Libya tattled, Pakistani army invaded Waziristan region. The army was surprised at local tribal backlash and adopted the line "Why are yoy fighting us, go and fight US in Afghanistan"

This line was supported by Afghan Taliban and practically every one of their leaders - emir Umar, Jalaluddin and Sirajuddin, Usmani, Daddullah Lang, Ubaidullah, chief justice NurMohammad Saqib, Mohammad Yasir, Sadar Azam - mediated on Pakistan's behalf with Waziristan insurgency.

However US intelligence (CIA) infiltrated Pakistan in 2005, using earthquake relief and humanitarian aid as a cover. Their targets were not only Taliban or Qaeda but also Pakistani groups linked to Kashmir, such as Taiba

Since the late 1990s US stance on Kashmir had been "Pakistan should forget Kashmir, make peace with India, and focus on its own Islamic radicalism", and this is what US (and their local lapdogs in Pakistan) pursued.

US also repeatedly sabotaged deals and ceasefires that, mediated by Taliban, army had made with insurgency in Waziristan. Ie it was US, not Taliban, who put Pakistan army and civilians at risk; it was US, not Taliban, who undermined Kashmir issue

Despite this Pakistan scrambled to appease and placate US, who would constantly bark "Do more" and undermine Pakistan further. It wasnt until 2011 that CIA agents were expelled as US/Pak relations hit a low and by then US was cracking down on Kashmir activists too

Meanwhile despite the Pak/US relations various Taliban networks incl Sirajuddin Haqqani continued to hit India; e.g. in 2008 Indian attache Ravi Mehta was killed at Kabul in a joint op w/ Taiba.

Sirajuddin was particularly hated by US for his role in a "triple agent" trick that killed CIA field commander Jennifer Matthews in what is still regarded as one of the most humiliating episodes in CIA history.

One CIA agent from this period - when agency was systematically destabilizing Pakistan, vilifying Kashmir, and bolstering India - was Sarah Adams, who has a particular visceral hatred for Sirajuddin Haqqani even by the agency's standards

Now bear in mind Adams' background. She was working for CIA in region at a time when it was actively involved in what even the most charitable appraisal could call anti-Pakistan/anti-Kashmir activity, during a period when Taliban were doing what Pakistan requested incl. v India.

however, after 2022 Pak coup - which was at least partly done for US approval - Pakistani pseudo-nationalists have embraced Adams as an unimpeachable source whenever she blames Taliban for anything, bcos they are eager to offload any responsibility onto "those ingrate Afghans"

Adams blames Taliban for everything, and she does this in large part bcos of her background and her resentment at Haqqanis, in particular. In 2023 she claimed that Sirajuddin had masterminded Hamas/Hezbollah war v Israel (oh, of course she also hates Palestine & loves Israel)

Adams doesn't give a shit abt Kashmir. She is pro-Israel, and Israel is pro-India, and India is anti-Kashmir. Her CIA career was spent partly in pro-India/anti-Kashmir activity. However, she knows that Pakistanis care about Kashmir and she wants to turn Pakistanis against Taliban

so after pro-US 2022 coup in Pakistan, Adams gets onto podcasts and "exposes" her target: "Oh, Taliban betrayed Kashmiris; oh, Taliban backstabbed Pakistan". If she could, she'd accuse Taliban of murdering Liaquat Ali-Khan in 1951 in order to whip up anti-Afghan sentiment in Pak

The fact is Taliban have a long record of supporting Pakistan and even Kashmir, and a looong record of opposing India at least on this issue. Whereas US,and its agents like Adams, have exactly the opposite record. But to justify current Pak war v Afg, she spreads these daft tales

Also, Taliban government is more or less the same ppl, with a handful of younger faces, as it was in 1996-2001 when they supported Pakistan. None of these people were anti-Pakistan until Pakistan, after the 2022 coup, started bombarding them."

text:
7. Buying and selling firecrackers: Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (What I see is that buying and selling them is Haram (prohibited), and that is for two reasons. The first reason: it is a waste of money, and wasting money is prohibited due to the Prophet's, peace and blessings be upon him, forbidding of that. The second: it causes harm to people with its disturbing noises, and it may cause fires if it falls on something flammable while it is alive [lit] and not extinguished.)  Majmoo Al-Fatawa (3/3).
> 
8. Al-Qaza': Which is shaving part of the head and leaving part of it: From Ibn Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both: (That the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, forbade Al-Qaza'.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5921), and Muslim (2120).
And from him, may Allah be pleased with him, he said: (The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saw a boy who had part of his hair shaved and part of it left, so he forbade them from that and said: 'Shave it all, or leave it all.')  Narrated by Abu Dawud (4195).
It is said in Al-Qamoos:
> (Al-Qaza' is to shave the head of a boy and leave separate spots un-shaved, resembling the Qaza' (scattered patches) of clouds, and similarly in An-Nihayah.)  Ghidha' Al-Albab (1/429).
> 
Sheikh Abdul Mohsen Al-Abbad, may Allah preserve him, said in his explanation of Sunan Abi Dawud:
> (And included in Al-Qaza' is what some youth do in barbershops by shaving the hair at the nape of the neck seeking adornment; because shaving part of the head and leaving part of it leads to the same result, whether it is intended for adornment or not intended for adornment, except if the shaving is for medical treatment or for Hijamah (cupping), then there is no harm in this.)
> 
9. Girls wearing short clothing:
Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (I see that a person should not dress his daughter in this short clothing while she is young, because if she gets used to it, she will remain upon it and its matter will become trivial to her. As for if she is accustomed to modesty from her youth, she will remain in that state in her adulthood. And what I advise our Muslim sisters is to abandon the clothing of the people abroad from the enemies of the religion, and to accustom their daughters to covering clothing, and to modesty, for modesty is from faith.)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (12/274).
> 
10. Not listening to the Eid sermon: Some people, after praying the Eid prayer, leave and do not listen to the sermon, and this is a mistake. Imam Malik was asked about a man who prays with the Imam on the day of Al-Fitr, is it permissible for him to leave before hearing the sermon? He said: (He should not leave until the Imam leaves.)  Ahkam Al-'Idayn by Al-Faryabi (144).
 There are many violations that occur during Eid; we have mentioned some of them. I ask Allah to benefit me and you through them, and Allah knows best.

"The West must dismantle the Muslim Brotherhood in full, not just monitor it, not just debate it. Dissolve it. Shut down its networks, charities, and political fronts.

Countries like Ireland, the UK, the US, France, and Germany must follow the example set by the UAE and Jordan. These arent just foreign policy moves, theyre acts of national defense.

Every day the Muslim Brotherhood is allowed to operate is a day it recruits, infiltrates, and rewrites the rules from within. This is not an organization with a place in civil society. It is a threat engineered to exploit it.

The UAE labeled the Muslim Brotherhood a terrorist organization and removed it from every institution, educational, religious, financial, and civic. Jordan went even further: the Brotherhood formally dissolved itself after losing all political and legal ground. These were not gestures. They were deliberate state decisions to eliminate the roots of radicalism before they could bloom into violence.

The same clarity is needed across the West, before the same patterns take hold in new soil",

text:
" The bid'ah of the Murji'ah is more harmful than that of the Khawarij. 

The scholars of Ahl al-Sunnah warned strongly about the bid'ah of Irja, to the extent that many of the Salaf considered it more dangerous than the innovation of the Khawarij.

Why?

 The Khawarij reach a limit in misguidance, but the Murji'ah have no boundary; they gradually strip the religion away, piece by piece.

 The Murji'ah open the door to zandaqah (heresy) and disbelief, by emptying iman of action.

 They corrupt the understanding of the religion while appearing outwardly agreeable, even aligning with rulers and worldly interests.

 They widen the door to false interpretations, distorting the texts without restraint.

 They give false security to sinners, claiming salvation despite wrongdoing, resembling the Jews who said:

  "The Fire will not touch us except for a number of days." (al-Baqarah: 80)

Because of this, many of the Salaf (such as al-Nakha'i) said:

"Fitnat al-Murji'ah is more feared upon this Ummah than that of the Azariqah (extreme Khawarij)."

And others said:

"The Khawarij are more excusable to me than the Murji'ah."

The reality is that both groups are harmful, but:

 The Khawarij corrupt through extremism and rebellion.
 The Murjiah corrupt the very foundation of iman itself.

And corruption of the foundation is more severe.

 al-Khorasaniya - Shaykh 'Abd al-Aziz al-Turayfi"

This lineage comes from the ancient Near East, especially regions like the Levant and Arabia, and is commonly linked to Semitic populations.

 Mothers lineage (mtDNA): H1
This is a typical Western European lineage, especially common in Iberia, showing local ancestry on the maternal side.

 Overall Ancestry

His genome shows a strong connection to eastern Mediterranean populations, especially groups historically related to:

Jewish communities of North Africa

Southern Europe (Italy, Greece)

His deeper ancestry is made up of:

Early farmers from Anatolia (Turkey region)

Significant Levantine/Natufian ancestry (ancient Near Eastern hunter-gatherers)

Smaller inputs from Iranian farmers, Caucasus groups, and European hunter-gatherers

What This Means

This man was not genetically Visigothic in the northern European sense.

Instead, he represents a mixed Mediterranean profile:

A Near Eastern paternal line

A local Iberian maternal line

A genome similar to eastern Mediterranean / North African Jewish populations

 Likely Historical Scenario

He was probably descended from people who moved west during the Roman Empire, such as:

Soldiers

Traders

Migrants from the eastern Mediterranean

These groups settled in Iberia and mixed with local populations. By the Visigothic period, their descendants were fully integrated culturally, even if their genetic origins were diverse.

 Key Insight

The term Visigothic here reflects culture and political identity, not a uniform genetic background.
This individual is a clear example of how migration and mixing in the Roman world shaped the population of early medieval Spain.

text:
"Ben Mermarli, lawyer of Marwan Barghouti, writes:

On April 12, 2026, I visited Marwan Barghouti in my capacity as his lawyer.

What I confirmed during this visit is deeply alarming. In recent weeks, Marwan Barghouti has been subjected to three violent assaults. On April 8, in Ganot Prison, he was severely beaten and left bleeding for more than two hours. He requested medical care, but his request was denied. On March 25, he was assaulted while being transferred from Megiddo to Ganot. And on March 24, in Megiddo Prison, guards entered his cell accompanied by a dog, forced him to lie on the ground, and the dog attacked him repeatedly.

These are not isolated incidents, but rather a clear pattern of escalating violations: violence, medical neglect, and treatment that places him in serious danger.

He had a lot to say. Above all, he wanted to know more about his family and the Palestinian people, and about what is happening on both the Palestinian and Israeli sides. I tried to update him on everything I knew.

But even that conversation took place under absurd conditions: the phones were not working, so we had to shout through the glass just to hear each other.

I sat there for five hours without food or water, trying to make sure that this visit was meaningful.

This is what a legal visit looks like today: deprivation of basic rights, obstruction of communication, and disregard for even the most basic human and professional standards.

And yet, despite all of this, his mind remained sharp, focused, and fully engaged with everything happening beyond the prison walls."

text:
Sincerity

Abu Bakr at-Tutl (d.270H) said:

Know that among the foremost things upon a servant is that he make all of his deeds purely for Allah. Much can be said on this topic, but its foundation, sincerity in ones actions, is this:

That you do a deed seeking only Allah by it, and you do not like that anyone among people should come to know of it. So if someone does happen to see your deed, you dislike that in your heart; you are not pleased by it, and you do not want anyone to praise you for anything you have done. Nor do you use it to gain status or standing with them. This is the foundation of sincerity in action, Allah willing and Allah is the One whose help is sought.

[at-Tutl, Kitb az-Zuhd]

"When a Scholar Makes an Error, the Whole World Makes an Error!

Sh Bad-ud-dn writes in the Muqaddimah of his Tafsr after explaining that requests were made of him to author a tafsr:

"So I began to ponder over the affair, and consider what I had to offer and the reality of the task at hand. If they had entrusted me to move a towering mountain, it would have been easier for me. I took a step forward and back again.

Another consideration: man is prone to making mistakes and forgetting, and I feared that I would make a mistake or an error, for when a scholar makes an error, the whole world makes an error, to the point that I intended to excuse myself."

text:
"Shaykh Abdullah Ibn Jibrn   

Long after his death, Shaykh Slih al-Fawzn (the current Muft) wrote a beautiful biographical tribute to Sheikh Abdullah bin Jibrn

He writes: He (Ibn Jibrn) was steadfast upon the methodology of the Pious Predecessors (Salaf as-Salih) in knowledge, action, and following their example, until his time came and the Muslims lost him and wept for him

P.s. Always remember the was a group who never wept for him, but actually celebrated when he was gone because they thought he was Ikhwani. Far away they are from Shaykh Fawzn who they claim to follow."
